translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Solomon the son of David was firmly established in his kingdom, and Yahweh  his God  was with him, and made him exceedingly great.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Solomon spoke to all Israel, to the captains of thousands and of hundreds, to the judges, and to every prince in all Israel, the heads of the fathers’ households.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Solomon, and all the assembly with him, went to the high place that was at Gibeon; for God’s Tent of Meeting was there, which Yahweh’s servant Moses had made in the wilderness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But David had brought God’s ark up from Kiriath Jearim to the place that David had prepared for it; for he had pitched a tent for it at Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover the bronze altar that Bezalel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, had made was there before Yahweh’s tabernacle; and Solomon and the assembly were seeking counsel there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Solomon went up there to the bronze altar before Yahweh, which was at the Tent of Meeting, and offered one thousand burnt offerings on it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That night, God appeared to Solomon and said to him, “Ask for what you want me to give you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Solomon said to God, “You have shown great loving kindness to David my father, and have made me king in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now, Yahweh God, let your promise to David my father be established; for you have made me king over a people like the dust of the earth in multitude.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now give me wisdom and knowledge, that I may go out and come in before this people; for who can judge this great people of yours?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God said to Solomon, “Because this was in your heart, and you have not asked riches, wealth, honor, or the life of those who hate you, nor yet have you asked for long life; but have asked for wisdom and knowledge for yourself, that you may judge my people, over whom I have made you king,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: therefore wisdom and knowledge is granted to you. I will give you riches, wealth, and honor, such as none of the kings have had who have been before you had, and none after you will have.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Solomon came from the high place that was at Gibeon, from before the Tent of Meeting, to Jerusalem; and he reigned over Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Solomon gathered chariots and horsemen. He had one thousand four hundred chariots and twelve thousand horsemen that he placed in the chariot cities, and with the king at Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king made silver and gold to be as common as stones in Jerusalem, and he made cedars to be as common as the sycamore trees that are in the lowland.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The horses which Solomon had were brought out of Egypt and from Kue. The king’s merchants purchased them from Kue.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They brought up and brought out of Egypt a chariot for six hundred pieces of silver, and a horse for one hundred fifty.  They also exported them to the Hittite kings and the Syrian  kings.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Solomon decided to build a house for Yahweh’s name, and a house for his kingdom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Solomon counted out seventy thousand men to bear burdens, eighty thousand men who were stone cutters in the mountains, and three thousand six hundred to oversee them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Solomon sent to Huram the king of Tyre, saying, “As you dealt with David my father, and sent him cedars to build him a house in which to dwell, so deal with me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold,  I am about to build a house for the name of Yahweh my God, to dedicate it to him, to burn before him incense of sweet spices, for the continual show bread, and for the burnt offerings morning and evening, on the Sabbaths, on the new moons, and on the set feasts of Yahweh our God. This is an ordinance forever to Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “The house which I am building will be great; for our God is greater than all gods.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But who is able to build him a house, since heaven and the heaven of heavens can’t contain him? Who am I then, that I should build him a house, except just to burn incense before him?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now therefore send me a man skillful to work in gold, in silver, in brass, in iron, and in purple, crimson, and blue, and who knows how to engrave engravings, to be with the skillful men who are with me in Judah and in Jerusalem, whom David my father provided.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Send me also cedar trees, cypress trees, and algum trees out of Lebanon; for I know that your servants know how to cut timber in Lebanon. Behold, my servants will be with your servants,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: even to prepare me timber in abundance; for the house which I am about to build will be great and wonderful.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, I will give to your servants, the cutters who cut timber, twenty thousand cors  of beaten wheat, twenty thousand baths  of barley, twenty thousand baths of wine, and twenty thousand baths of oil.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Huram the king of Tyre answered in writing, which he sent to Solomon, “Because Yahweh loves his people, he has made you king over them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Huram continued, “Blessed be Yahweh, the God of Israel, who made heaven and earth, who has given to David the king a wise son, endowed with discretion and understanding, who would build a house for Yahweh, and a house for his kingdom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now I have sent a skillful man, endowed with understanding, of Huram my father’s,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the son of a woman of the daughters of Dan; and his father was a man of Tyre, skillful to work in gold, in silver, in brass, in iron, in stone, in timber, and in purple, in blue, in fine linen, and in crimson, also to engrave any kind of engraving and to devise any device; that there may be a place appointed to him with your skillful men, and with the skillful men of my lord David your father.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now therefore the wheat the barley, the oil and the wine, which my lord has spoken of, let him send to his servants;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and we will cut wood out of Lebanon, as much as you need. We will bring it to you in floats by sea to Joppa; and you shall carry it up to Jerusalem.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Solomon counted all the foreigners who were in the land of Israel, after the census with which David his father had counted them; and they found one hundred fifty-three thousand six hundred.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He set seventy thousand of them to bear burdens, eighty thousand who were stone cutters in the mountains, and three thousand six hundred overseers to assign the people their work.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Solomon began to build Yahweh’s house at Jerusalem on Mount Moriah, where Yahweh appeared to David his father, which he prepared in the place that David had appointed, on the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He began to build in the second day of the second month, in the fourth year of his reign.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now these are the foundations which Solomon laid for the building of God’s house. The length by cubits  after the first measure was sixty cubits, and the width twenty cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The porch that was in front, its length, according to the width of the house, was twenty cubits, and the height one hundred twenty; and he overlaid it within with pure gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the larger room with a ceiling of cypress wood, which he overlaid with fine gold, and ornamented it with palm trees and chains.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He decorated the house with precious stones for beauty. The gold was gold from Parvaim.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also overlaid the house, the beams, the thresholds, its walls, and its doors with gold; and engraved cherubim on the walls.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the most holy place. Its length, according to the width of the house, was twenty cubits, and its width twenty cubits; and he overlaid it with fine gold, amounting to six hundred talents.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The weight of the nails was fifty shekels  of gold. He overlaid the upper rooms with gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the most holy place he made two cherubim by carving; and they overlaid them with gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wings of the cherubim were twenty cubits long: the wing of the one was five cubits, reaching to the wall of the house; and the other wing was five cubits, reaching to the wing of the other cherub.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wing of the other cherub was five cubits, reaching to the wall of the house; and the other wing was five cubits, joining to the wing of the other cherub.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The wings of these cherubim spread themselves out twenty cubits. They stood on their feet, and their faces were toward the house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the veil of blue, purple, crimson, and fine linen, and ornamented it with cherubim.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also he made before the house two pillars of thirty-five cubits height, and the capital that was on the top of each of them was five cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made chains in the inner sanctuary, and put them on the tops of the pillars; and he made one hundred pomegranates, and put them on the chains.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He set up the pillars before the temple, one on the right hand, and the other on the left; and called the name of that on the right hand Jachin, and the name of that on the left Boaz.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he made an altar of brass, twenty cubits  long, twenty cubits wide, and ten cubits high.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also he made the molten sea  of ten cubits from brim to brim. It was round, five cubits high, and thirty cubits in circumference.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Under it was the likeness of oxen, which encircled it, for ten cubits, encircling the sea. The oxen were in two rows, cast when it was cast.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It stood on twelve oxen, three looking toward the north, and three looking toward the west, and three looking toward the south, and three looking toward the east; and the sea was set on them above, and all their hindquarters were inward.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It was a handbreadth thick; and its brim was made like the brim of a cup, like the flower of a lily. It received and held three thousand baths.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also made ten basins, and put five on the right hand, and five on the left, to wash in them. The things that belonged to the burnt offering were washed in them; but the sea was for the priests to wash in.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made the ten lamp stands of gold according to the ordinance concerning them; and he set them in the temple, five on the right hand, and five on the left.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made also ten tables, and placed them in the temple, five on the right side, and five on the left. He made one hundred basins of gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Furthermore he made the court of the priests, the great court, and doors for the court, and overlaid their doors with brass.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He set the sea on the right side of the house eastward, toward the south.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Huram made the pots, the shovels, and the basins. So Huram finished doing the work that he did for king Solomon in God’s house:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the two pillars, the bowls, the two capitals which were on the top of the pillars, the two networks to cover the two bowls of the capitals that were on the top of the pillars,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the four hundred pomegranates for the two networks; two rows of pomegranates for each network, to cover the two bowls of the capitals that were on the pillars.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also made the bases, and he made the basins on the bases;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: one sea, and the twelve oxen under it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Huram his father also made the pots, the shovels, the forks, and all its vessels for king Solomon, for Yahweh’s house, of bright brass.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king cast them in the plain of the Jordan, in the clay ground between Succoth and Zeredah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus Solomon made all these vessels in great abundance; for the weight of the brass could not be determined.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Solomon made all the vessels that were in God’s house, the golden altar also, and the tables with the show bread on them;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the lamp stands with their lamps, to burn according to the ordinance before the inner sanctuary, of pure gold;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the flowers, the lamps, and the tongs of gold that was perfect gold;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the snuffers, the basins, the spoons, and the fire pans of pure gold. As for the entry of the house, its inner doors for the most holy place and the doors of the main hall of the temple were of gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus all the work that Solomon did for Yahweh’s house was finished. Solomon brought in the things that David his father had dedicated, even the silver, the gold, and all the vessels, and put them in the treasuries of God’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Solomon assembled the elders of Israel, and all the heads of the tribes, the princes of the fathers’ households of the children of Israel, to Jerusalem, to bring up the ark of Yahweh’s covenant out of David’s city, which is Zion.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So all the men of Israel assembled themselves to the king at the feast, which was in the seventh month.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the elders of Israel came. The Levites took up the ark;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and they brought up the ark, the Tent of Meeting, and all the holy vessels that were in the Tent; these the priests the Levites brought up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: King Solomon and all the congregation of Israel, who were assembled to him, were before the ark, sacrificing sheep and cattle that could not be counted or counted for multitude.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The priests brought in the ark of Yahweh’s covenant to its place, into the inner sanctuary of the house, to the most holy place, even under the wings of the cherubim.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the cherubim spread out their wings over the place of the ark, and the cherubim covered the ark and its poles above.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The poles were so long that the ends of the poles were seen from the ark in front of the inner sanctuary; but they were not seen outside; and it is there to this day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There was nothing in the ark except the two tablets which Moses put at Horeb, when Yahweh made a covenant with the children of Israel, when they came out of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the priests had come out of the holy place (for all the priests who were present had sanctified themselves, and didn’t keep their divisions;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: also the Levites who were the singers, all of them, even Asaph, Heman, Jeduthun, and their sons and their brothers, arrayed in fine linen, with cymbals and stringed instruments and harps, stood at the east end of the altar, and with them one hundred twenty priests sounding with trumpets);

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: when the trumpeters and singers were as one, to make one sound to be heard in praising and thanking Yahweh; and when they lifted up their voice with the trumpets and cymbals and instruments of music, and praised Yahweh, saying, “For he is good; for his loving kindness endures forever!”  then the house was filled with a cloud, even Yahweh’s house,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: so that the priests could not stand to minister by reason of the cloud; for Yahweh’s glory filled God’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Solomon said, “Yahweh has said that he would dwell in the thick darkness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I have built you a house and home, a place for you to dwell in forever.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king turned his face, and blessed all the assembly of Israel: and all the assembly of Israel stood.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Blessed be Yahweh, the God of Israel, who spoke with his mouth to David my father, and has with his hands fulfilled it, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: ‘Since the day that I brought my people out of the land of Egypt, I chose no city out of all the tribes of Israel to build a house in, that my name might be there and I chose no man to be prince over my people Israel;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but now I have chosen Jerusalem, that my name might be there; and I have chosen David to be over my people Israel.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now it was in the heart of David my father to build a house for the name of Yahweh, the God of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Yahweh said to David my father, ‘Whereas it was in your heart to build a house for my name, you did well that it was in your heart;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: nevertheless you shall not build the house; but your son who will come out of your body, he shall build the house for my name.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Yahweh has performed his word that he spoke; for I have risen up in the place of David my father, and sit on the throne of Israel, as Yahweh promised, and have built the house for the name of Yahweh, the God of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There I have set the ark, in which is Yahweh’s covenant, which he made with the children of Israel.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He stood before Yahweh’s altar in the presence of all the assembly of Israel, and spread out his hands

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: (for Solomon had made a bronze scaffold, five cubits  long, and five cubits wide, and three cubits high, and had set it in the middle of the court; and he stood on it, and knelt down on his knees before all the assembly of Israel, and spread out his hands toward heaven)

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and he said, “Yahweh, the God of Israel, there is no God like you in heaven or on earth; you who keep covenant and loving kindness with your servants who walk before you with all their heart;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who have kept with your servant David my father that which you promised him. Yes, you spoke with your mouth, and have fulfilled it with your hand, as it is today.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now therefore, Yahweh, the God of Israel, keep with your servant David my father that which you have promised him, saying, ‘There shall not fail you a man in my sight to sit on the throne of Israel, if only your children take heed to their way, to walk in my law as you have walked before me.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore, Yahweh, the God of Israel, let your word be verified, which you spoke to your servant David.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But will God indeed dwell with men on the earth? Behold, heaven and the heaven of heavens can’t contain you; how much less this house which I have built!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yet have respect for the prayer of your servant, and to his supplication, Yahweh my God, to listen to the cry and to the prayer which your servant prays before you;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that your eyes may be open toward this house day and night, even toward the place where you have said that you would put your name; to listen to the prayer which your servant will pray toward this place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Listen to the petitions of your servant, and of your people Israel, when they pray toward this place. Yes, hear from your dwelling place, even from heaven; and when you hear, forgive.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If a man sins against his neighbor, and an oath is laid on him to cause him to swear, and he comes and swears before your altar in this house;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then hear from heaven, act, and judge your servants, bringing retribution to the wicked, to bring his way on his own head; and justifying the righteous, to give him according to his righteousness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If your people Israel are struck down before the enemy because they have sinned against you, and they turn again and confess your name, and pray and make supplication before you in this house;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then hear from heaven, and forgive the sin of your people Israel, and bring them again to the land which you gave to them and to their fathers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “When the sky is shut up, and there is no rain, because they have sinned against you; if they pray toward this place, and confess your name, and turn from their sin, when you afflict them;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then hear in heaven, and forgive the sin of your servants of your people Israel, when you teach them the good way in which they should walk; and send rain on your land, which you have given to your people for an inheritance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If there is famine in the land, if there is pestilence, if there is blight or mildew, locust or caterpillar; if their enemies besiege them in the land of their cities; whatever plague or whatever sickness there is;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: whatever prayer and supplication is made by any man, or by all your people Israel, who will each know his own plague and his own sorrow, and shall spread out his hands toward this house;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then hear from heaven your dwelling place and forgive, and render to every man according to all his ways, whose heart you know (for you, even you only, know the hearts of the children of men)

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that they may fear you, to walk in your ways, so long as they live in the land which you gave to our fathers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Moreover concerning the foreigner, who is not of your people Israel, when he comes from a far country for your great name’s sake, and your mighty hand, and your outstretched arm; when they come and pray toward this house;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then hear from heaven, even from your dwelling place, and do according to all that the foreigner calls to you for; that all the peoples of the earth may know your name and fear you, as do your people Israel, and that they may know that this house which I have built is called by your name.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If your people go out to battle against their enemies, by whatever way you send them, and they pray to you toward this city which you have chosen, and the house which I have built for your name;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then hear from heaven their prayer and their supplication, and maintain their cause.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If they sin against you (for there is no man who doesn’t sin), and you are angry with them, and deliver them to the enemy, so that they carry them away captive to a land far off or near;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: yet if they come to their senses in the land where they are carried captive, and turn again, and make supplication to you in the land of their captivity, saying, ‘We have sinned, we have done perversely, and have dealt wickedly;’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: if they return to you with all their heart and with all their soul in the land of their captivity, where they have carried them captive, and pray toward their land, which you gave to their fathers, and the city which you have chosen, and toward the house which I have built for your name;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then hear from heaven, even from your dwelling place, their prayer and their petitions, and maintain their cause, and forgive your people who have sinned against you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now, my God, let, I beg you, your eyes be open, and let your ears be attentive, to the prayer that is made in this place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now therefore arise, Yahweh God, into your resting place, you, and the ark of your strength. Let your priests, Yahweh God, be clothed with salvation, and let your saints rejoice in goodness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Yahweh God, don’t turn away the face of your anointed. Remember your loving kindnesses to David your servant.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now when Solomon had finished praying, fire came down from heaven and consumed the burnt offering and the sacrifices; and Yahweh’s glory filled the house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The priests could not enter into Yahweh’s house, because Yahweh’s glory filled Yahweh’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the children of Israel looked on, when the fire came down, and Yahweh’s glory was on the house. They bowed themselves with their faces to the ground on the pavement, worshiped, and gave thanks to Yahweh, saying, “For he is good; for his loving kindness endures for ever.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the king and all the people offered sacrifices before Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: King Solomon offered a sacrifice of twenty-two thousand head of cattle and a hundred twenty thousand sheep. So the king and all the people dedicated God’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The priests stood, according to their positions; the Levites also with instruments of music of Yahweh, which David the king had made to give thanks to Yahweh, when David praised by their ministry, saying “For his loving kindness endures for ever.” The priests sounded trumpets before them; and all Israel stood.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover Solomon made the middle of the court that was before Yahweh’s house holy; for there he offered the burnt offerings, and the fat of the peace offerings, because the bronze altar which Solomon had made was not able to receive the burnt offering, the meal offering, and the fat.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Solomon held the feast at that time for seven days, and all Israel with him, a very great assembly, from the entrance of Hamath to the brook of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the eighth day, they held a solemn assembly; for they kept the dedication of the altar seven days, and the feast seven days.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the twenty-third day of the seventh month, he sent the people away to their tents, joyful and glad of heart for the goodness that Yahweh had shown to David, and to Solomon, and to Israel his people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus Solomon finished Yahweh’s house and the king’s house; and he successfully completed all that came into Solomon’s heart to make in Yahweh’s house and in his own house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh appeared to Solomon by night, and said to him, “I have heard your prayer, and have chosen this place for myself for a house of sacrifice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If I shut up the sky so that there is no rain, or if I command the locust to devour the land, or if I send pestilence among my people;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: if my people, who are called by my name, will humble themselves, pray, seek my face, and turn from their wicked ways; then I will hear from heaven, will forgive their sin, and will heal their land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now my eyes will be open my ears attentive to prayer that is made in this place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For now I have chosen and made this house holy, that my name may be there forever; and my eyes and my heart will be there perpetually.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “As for you, if you will walk before me as David your father walked, and do according to all that I have commanded you, and will keep my statutes and my ordinances;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then I will establish the throne of your kingdom, according as I covenanted with David your father, saying, ‘There shall not fail you a man to be ruler in Israel.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But if you turn away, and forsake my statutes and my commandments which I have set before you, and shall go and serve other gods, and worship them;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then I will pluck them up by the roots out of my land which I have given them; and this house, which I have made holy for my name, I will cast out of my sight, and I will make it a proverb and a byword among all peoples.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This house, which is so high, everyone who passes by it shall be astonished, and shall say, ‘Why has Yahweh done this to this land and to this house?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They shall answer, ‘Because they abandoned Yahweh, the God of their fathers, who brought them out of the land of Egypt, and took other gods, worshiped them, and served them. Therefore he has brought all this evil on them.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At the end of twenty years, in which Solomon had built Yahweh’s house and his own house,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Solomon built the cities which Huram had given to Solomon, and caused the children of Israel to dwell there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Solomon went to Hamath Zobah, and prevailed against it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He built Tadmor in the wilderness, and all the storage cities, which he built in Hamath.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also he built Beth Horon the upper and Beth Horon the lower, fortified cities, with walls, gates, and bars;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and Baalath, and all the storage cities that Solomon had, and all the cities for his chariots, the cities for his horsemen, and all that Solomon desired to build for his pleasure in Jerusalem, in Lebanon, and in all the land of his dominion.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As for all the people who were left of the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites, who were not of Israel;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: of their children who were left after them in the land, whom the children of Israel didn’t consume, of them Solomon conscripted forced labor to this day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But of the children of Israel, Solomon made no servants for his work; but they were men of war, and chief of his captains, and rulers of his chariots and of his horsemen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These were the chief officers of king Solomon, even two-hundred fifty, who ruled over the people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Solomon brought up Pharaoh’s daughter out of David’s city to the house that he had built for her; for he said, “My wife shall not dwell in the house of David king of Israel, because the places where Yahweh’s ark has come are holy.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Solomon offered burnt offerings to Yahweh on Yahweh’s altar, which he had built before the porch,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: even as the duty of every day required, offering according to the commandment of Moses, on the Sabbaths, on the new moons, and on the set feasts, three times per year, during the feast of unleavened bread, during the feast of weeks, and during the feast of tents.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He appointed, according to the ordinance of David his father, the divisions of the priests to their service, and the Levites to their offices, to praise and to minister before the priests, as the duty of every day required; the doorkeepers also by their divisions at every gate, for David the man of God had so commanded.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They didn’t depart from the commandment of the king to the priests and Levites concerning any matter, or concerning the treasures.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now all the work of Solomon was prepared from the day of the foundation of Yahweh’s house until it was finished. So Yahweh’s house was completed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Solomon went to Ezion Geber and to Eloth, on the seashore in the land of Edom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Huram sent him ships and servants who had knowledge of the sea by the hands of his servants; and they came with the servants of Solomon to Ophir, and brought from there four hundred fifty talents  of gold, and brought them to king Solomon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the queen of Sheba heard of the fame of Solomon, she came to test Solomon with hard questions at Jerusalem, with a very great caravan, including camels that bore spices, gold in abundance, and precious stones. When she had come to Solomon, she talked with him about all that was in her heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Solomon answered all her questions. There wasn’t anything hidden from Solomon which he didn’t tell her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the queen of Sheba had seen the wisdom of Solomon, the house that he had built,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the food of his table, the seating of his servants, the attendance of his ministers, their clothing, his cup bearers also, their clothing, and his ascent by which he went up to Yahweh’s house; there was no more spirit in her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She said to the king, “It was a true report that I heard in my own land of your acts and of your wisdom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: However I didn’t believe their words until I came, and my eyes had seen it; and behold, the half of the greatness of your wisdom was not told me. You exceed the fame that I heard!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Happy are your men, and happy are these your servants, who stand continually before you, and hear your wisdom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed be Yahweh your God, who delighted in you, to set you on his throne, to be king for Yahweh your God; because your God loved Israel, to establish them forever. Therefore made he you king over them, to do justice and righteousness.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She gave the king one hundred and twenty talents  of gold, spices in great abundance, and precious stones. There was never before such spice as the queen of Sheba gave to king Solomon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The servants of Huram and the servants of Solomon, who brought gold from Ophir, also brought algum trees  and precious stones.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king used algum tree wood to make terraces for Yahweh’s house and for the king’s house, and harps and stringed instruments for the singers. There were none like these seen before in the land of Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: King Solomon gave to the queen of Sheba all her desire, whatever she asked, besides that which she had brought to the king. So she turned, and went to her own land, she and her servants.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the weight of gold that came to Solomon in one year was six hundred sixty-six talents  of gold,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: besides that which the traders and merchants brought. All the kings of Arabia and the governors of the country brought gold and silver to Solomon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: King Solomon made two hundred bucklers of beaten gold. Six hundred shekels  of beaten gold went to one buckler.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He made three hundred shields of beaten gold. Three hundred shekels  of gold went to one shield. The king put them in the House of the Forest of Lebanon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover the king made a great throne of ivory, and overlaid it with pure gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There were six steps to the throne, with a footstool of gold, which were fastened to the throne, and armrests on either side by the place of the seat, and two lions standing beside the armrests.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Twelve lions stood there on the one side and on the other on the six steps. There was nothing like it made in any other kingdom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All king Solomon’s drinking vessels were of gold, and all the vessels of the House of the Forest of Lebanon were of pure gold. Silver was not considered valuable in the days of Solomon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the king had ships that went to Tarshish with Huram’s servants. Once every three years, the ships of Tarshish came bringing gold, silver, ivory, apes, and peacocks.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So king Solomon exceeded all the kings of the earth in riches and wisdom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the kings of the earth sought the presence of Solomon, to hear his wisdom, which God had put in his heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They each brought tribute, vessels of silver, vessels of gold, clothing, armor, spices, horses, and mules every year.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Solomon had four thousand stalls for horses and chariots, and twelve thousand horsemen, that he stationed in the chariot cities, and with the king at Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He ruled over all the kings from the River even to the land of the Philistines, and to the border of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king made silver as common in Jerusalem as stones, and he made cedars to be as abundant as the sycamore trees that are in the lowland.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They brought horses for Solomon out of Egypt and out of all lands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the rest of the acts of Solomon, first and last, aren’t they written in the history of Nathan the prophet, and in the prophecy of Ahijah the Shilonite, and in the visions of Iddo the seer concerning Jeroboam the son of Nebat?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Solomon reigned in Jerusalem over all Israel forty years.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Solomon slept with his fathers, and he was buried in his father David’s city: and Rehoboam his son reigned in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Rehoboam went to Shechem; for all Israel had come to Shechem to make him king.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Jeroboam the son of Nebat heard of it (for he was in Egypt, where he had fled from the presence of king Solomon), Jeroboam returned out of Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They sent and called him; and Jeroboam and all Israel came, and they spoke to Rehoboam, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Your father made our yoke grievous: now therefore make the grievous service of your father, and his heavy yoke which he put on us, lighter, and we will serve you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Come again to me after three days.” So the people departed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: King Rehoboam took counsel with the old men, who had stood before Solomon his father while he yet lived, saying, “What counsel do you give me about how to answer these people?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They spoke to him, saying, “If you are kind to these people, please them, and speak good words to them, then they will be your servants forever.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he abandoned the counsel of the old men which they had given him, and took counsel with the young men who had grown up with him, who stood before him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “What counsel do you give, that we may give an answer to these people, who have spoken to me, saying, ‘Make the yoke that your father put on us lighter?’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The young men who had grown up with him spoke to him, saying, “Thus you shall tell the people who spoke to you, saying, ‘Your father made our yoke heavy, but make it lighter on us;’ thus you shall say to them, ‘My little finger is thicker than my father’s waist.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now whereas my father burdened you with a heavy yoke, I will add to your yoke. My father chastised you with whips, but I will chastise you with scorpions.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Jeroboam and all the people came to Rehoboam the third day, as the king asked, saying, “Come to me again the third day.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king answered them roughly; and king Rehoboam abandoned the counsel of the old men,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and spoke to them after the counsel of the young men, saying, “My father made your yoke heavy, but I will add to it. My father chastised you with whips, but I will chastise you with scorpions.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the king didn’t listen to the people; for it was brought about by God, that Yahweh might establish his word, which he spoke by Ahijah the Shilonite to Jeroboam the son of Nebat.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When all Israel saw that the king didn’t listen to them, the people answered the king, saying, “What portion have we in David? We don’t have an inheritance in the son of Jesse! Every man to your tents, Israel! Now see to your own house, David.” So all Israel departed to their tents.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But as for the children of Israel who lived in the cities of Judah, Rehoboam reigned over them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then king Rehoboam sent Hadoram, who was over the men subject to forced labor; and the children of Israel stoned him to death with stones. King Rehoboam hurried to get himself up to his chariot, to flee to Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Israel rebelled against David’s house to this day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Rehoboam had come to Jerusalem, he assembled the house of Judah and Benjamin, one hundred eighty thousand chosen men who were warriors, to fight against Israel, to bring the kingdom again to Rehoboam.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Yahweh’s word came to Shemaiah the man of God, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Speak to Rehoboam the son of Solomon, king of Judah, and to all Israel in Judah and Benjamin, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: ‘Yahweh says, “You shall not go up, nor fight against your brothers! Every man return to his house; for this thing is of me.”’” So they listened to Yahweh’s words, and returned from going against Jeroboam.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Rehoboam lived in Jerusalem, and built cities for defense in Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He built Bethlehem, Etam, Tekoa,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Beth Zur, Soco, Adullam,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Gath, Mareshah, Ziph,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Adoraim, Lachish, Azekah,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Zorah, Aijalon, and Hebron, which are fortified cities in Judah and in Benjamin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He fortified the strongholds, and put captains in them, and stores of food, oil and wine.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He put shields and spears in every city, and made them exceedingly strong. Judah and Benjamin belonged to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The priests and the Levites who were in all Israel stood with him out of all their territory.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Levites left their suburbs and their possession, and came to Judah and Jerusalem; for Jeroboam and his sons cast them off, that they should not execute the priest’s office to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He himself appointed priests for the high places, for the male goats, and for the calves which he had made.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After them, out of all the tribes of Israel, those who set their hearts to seek Yahweh, the God of Israel, came to Jerusalem to sacrifice to Yahweh, the God of their fathers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So they strengthened the kingdom of Judah, and made Rehoboam the son of Solomon strong for three years; for they walked three years in the way of David and Solomon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Rehoboam took a wife for himself, Mahalath the daughter of Jerimoth the son of David and of Abihail the daughter of Eliab the son of Jesse.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She bore him sons: Jeush, Shemariah, and Zaham.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After her, he took Maacah the daughter of Absalom; and she bore him Abijah, Attai, Ziza, and Shelomith.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Rehoboam loved Maacah the daughter of Absalom above all his wives and his concubines; for he took eighteen wives and sixty concubines, and became the father of twenty-eight sons and sixty daughters.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Rehoboam appointed Abijah the son of Maacah to be chief, the prince among his brothers; for he intended to make him king.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He dealt wisely, and dispersed of all his sons throughout all the lands of Judah and Benjamin, to every fortified city. He gave them food in abundance and he sought many wives for them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the kingdom of Rehoboam was established and he was strong, he abandoned Yahweh’s law, and all Israel with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the fifth year of king Rehoboam, Shishak king of Egypt came up against Jerusalem, because they had trespassed against Yahweh,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: with twelve hundred chariots, and sixty thousand horsemen. The people were without number who came with him out of Egypt: the Lubim, the Sukkiim, and the Ethiopians.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took the fortified cities which belonged to Judah, and came to Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Shemaiah the prophet came to Rehoboam, and to the princes of Judah, who were gathered together to Jerusalem because of Shishak, and said to them, “Yahweh says, ‘You have forsaken me, therefore I have also left you in the hand of Shishak.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the princes of Israel and the king humbled themselves; and they said, “Yahweh is righteous.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Yahweh saw that they humbled themselves, Yahweh’s word came to Shemaiah, saying, “They have humbled themselves. I will not destroy them; but I will grant them some deliverance, and my wrath won’t be poured out on Jerusalem by the hand of Shishak.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless they will be his servants, that they may know my service, and the service of the kingdoms of the countries.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Shishak king of Egypt came up against Jerusalem and took away the treasures of Yahweh’s house and the treasures of the king’s house. He took it all away. He also took away the shields of gold which Solomon had made.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: King Rehoboam made shields of brass in their place, and committed them to the hands of the captains of the guard, who kept the door of the king’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As often as the king entered into Yahweh’s house, the guard came and bore them, then brought them back into the guard room.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he humbled himself, Yahweh’s wrath turned from him, so as not to destroy him altogether. Moreover, there were good things found in Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So king Rehoboam strengthened himself in Jerusalem and reigned; for Rehoboam was forty-one years old when he began to reign, and he reigned seventeen years in Jerusalem, the city which Yahweh had chosen out of all the tribes of Israel to put his name there. His mother’s name was Naamah the Ammonitess.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He did that which was evil, because he didn’t set his heart to seek Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the acts of Rehoboam, first and last, aren’t they written in the histories of Shemaiah the prophet and of Iddo the seer, in the genealogies? There were wars between Rehoboam and Jeroboam continually.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Rehoboam slept with his fathers, and was buried in David’s city; and Abijah his son reigned in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the eighteenth year of king Jeroboam, Abijah began to reign over Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He reigned three years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Micaiah the daughter of Uriel of Gibeah. There was war between Abijah and Jeroboam.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Abijah joined battle with an army of valiant men of war, even four hundred thousand chosen men; and Jeroboam set the battle in array against him with eight hundred thousand chosen men, who were mighty men of valor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Abijah stood up on Mount Zemaraim, which is in the hill country of Ephraim, and said, “Hear me, Jeroboam and all Israel:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ought you not to know that Yahweh, the God of Israel, gave the kingdom over Israel to David forever, even to him and to his sons by a covenant of salt?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yet Jeroboam the son of Nebat, the servant of Solomon the son of David, rose up, and rebelled against his lord.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Worthless men were gathered to him, base fellows who strengthened themselves against Rehoboam the son of Solomon, when Rehoboam was young and tender hearted, and could not withstand them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now you intend to withstand the kingdom of Yahweh in the hand of the sons of David. You are a great multitude, and the golden calves which Jeroboam made you for gods are with you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Haven’t you driven out the priests of Yahweh, the sons of Aaron, and the Levites, and made priests for yourselves according to the ways of the peoples of other lands? Whoever comes to consecrate himself with a young bull and seven rams may be a priest of those who are no gods.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But as for us, Yahweh is our God, and we have not forsaken him. We have priests serving Yahweh, the sons of Aaron, and the Levites in their work;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and they burn to Yahweh every morning and every evening burnt offerings and sweet incense. They also set the show bread in order on the pure table; and the lamp stand of gold with its lamps, to burn every evening; for we keep the instruction of Yahweh our God, but you have forsaken him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, God is with us at our head, and his priests with the trumpets of alarm to sound an alarm against you. Children of Israel, don’t fight against Yahweh, the God of your fathers; for you will not prosper.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jeroboam caused an ambush to come about behind them; so they were before Judah, and the ambush was behind them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Judah looked back, behold, the battle was before and behind them; and they cried to Yahweh, and the priests sounded with the trumpets.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the men of Judah gave a shout. As the men of Judah shouted, God struck Jeroboam and all Israel before Abijah and Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Israel fled before Judah, and God delivered them into their hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Abijah and his people killed them with a great slaughter, so five hundred thousand chosen men of Israel fell down slain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus the children of Israel were brought under at that time, and the children of Judah prevailed, because they relied on Yahweh, the God of their fathers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Abijah pursued Jeroboam, and took cities from him, Bethel with its villages, Jeshanah with its villages, and Ephron with its villages.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jeroboam didn’t recover strength again in the days of Abijah. Yahweh struck him, and he died.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Abijah grew mighty, and took for himself fourteen wives, and became the father of twenty-two sons, and sixteen daughters.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The rest of the acts of Abijah, his ways, and his sayings are written in the commentary of the prophet Iddo.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Abijah slept with his fathers, and they buried him in David’s city; and Asa his son reigned in his place. In his days, the land was quiet ten years.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Asa did that which was good and right in Yahweh his God’s eyes;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for he took away the foreign altars and the high places, broke down the pillars, cut down the Asherah poles,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and commanded Judah to seek Yahweh, the God of their fathers, and to obey his law and command.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also he took away out of all the cities of Judah the high places and the sun images; and the kingdom was quiet before him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He built fortified cities in Judah; for the land was quiet, and he had no war in those years, because Yahweh had given him rest.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he said to Judah, “Let us build these cities, and make walls around them, with towers, gates, and bars. The land is yet before us, because we have sought Yahweh our God. We have sought him, and he has given us rest on every side.” So they built and prospered.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Asa had an army of three hundred thousand out of Judah who bore bucklers and spears, and two hundred eighty thousand out of Benjamin who bore shields and drew bows. All these were mighty men of valor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Zerah the Ethiopian came out against them with an army of a million troops and three hundred chariots, and he came to Mareshah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Asa went out to meet him, and they set the battle in array in the valley of Zephathah at Mareshah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Asa cried to Yahweh his God, and said, “Yahweh, there is no one besides you to help, between the mighty and him who has no strength. Help us, Yahweh our God; for we rely on you, and in your name are we come against this multitude. Yahweh, you are our God. Don’t let man prevail against you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Yahweh struck the Ethiopians before Asa and before Judah; and the Ethiopians fled.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Asa and the people who were with him pursued them to Gerar: and so many of the Ethiopians fell that they could not recover themselves; for they were destroyed before Yahweh and before his army; and they carried away very much booty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They struck all the cities around Gerar; for the fear of Yahweh came on them, and they plundered all the cities; for there was much plunder in them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They also struck the tents of livestock, and carried away sheep in abundance, and camels, and returned to Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Spirit of God came on Azariah the son of Oded:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and he went out to meet Asa, and said to him, “Hear me, Asa, and all Judah and Benjamin! Yahweh is with you, while you are with him; and if you seek him, he will be found by you; but if you forsake him, he will forsake you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now for a long time Israel was without the true God, without a teaching priest, and without law.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when in their distress they turned to Yahweh, the God of Israel, and sought him, he was found by them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In those times there was no peace to him who went out, nor to him who came in; but great troubles were on all the inhabitants of the lands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They were broken in pieces, nation against nation, and city against city; for God troubled them with all adversity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But you be strong, and don’t let your hands be slack; for your work will be rewarded.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Asa heard these words, and the prophecy of Oded the prophet, he took courage, and put away the abominations out of all the land of Judah and Benjamin, and out of the cities which he had taken from the hill country of Ephraim; and he renewed Yahweh’s altar that was before Yahweh’s porch.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He gathered all Judah and Benjamin, and those who lived with them out of Ephraim, Manasseh, and Simeon; for they came to him out of Israel in abundance when they saw that Yahweh his God was with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So they gathered themselves together at Jerusalem in the third month, in the fifteenth year of Asa’s reign.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They sacrificed to Yahweh in that day, of the plunder which they had brought, seven hundred head of cattle and seven thousand sheep.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They entered into the covenant to seek Yahweh, the God of their fathers, with all their heart and with all their soul;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and that whoever would not seek Yahweh, the God of Israel, should be put to death, whether small or great, whether man or woman.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They swore to Yahweh with a loud voice, with shouting, with trumpets, and with cornets.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All Judah rejoiced at the oath, for they had sworn with all their heart, and sought him with their whole desire; and he was found by them. Then Yahweh gave them rest all around.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also Maacah, the mother of Asa the king, he removed from being queen, because she had made an abominable image for an Asherah; so Asa cut down her image, ground it into dust, and burned it at the brook Kidron.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the high places were not taken away out of Israel; nevertheless the heart of Asa was perfect all his days.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He brought the things that his father had dedicated, and that he himself had dedicated, silver, gold, and vessels into God’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There was no more war to the thirty-fifth year of Asa’s reign.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the thirty-sixth year of Asa’s reign, Baasha king of Israel went up against Judah, and built Ramah, that he might not allow anyone to go out or come in to Asa king of Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Asa brought out silver and gold out of the treasures of Yahweh’s house and of the king’s house, and sent to Ben Hadad king of Syria, who lived at Damascus, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Let there be a treaty between me and you, as there was between my father and your father. Behold, I have sent you silver and gold. Go, break your treaty with Baasha king of Israel, that he may depart from me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ben Hadad listened to king Asa, and sent the captains of his armies against the cities of Israel; and they struck Ijon, Dan, Abel Maim, and all the storage cities of Naphtali.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Baasha heard of it, he stopped building Ramah, and let his work cease.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Asa the king took all Judah, and they carried away the stones of Rama, and its timber, with which Baasha had built; and he built Geba and Mizpah with them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At that time Hanani the seer came to Asa king of Judah, and said to him, “Because you have relied on the king of Syria, and have not relied on Yahweh your God, therefore the army of the king of Syria has escaped out of your hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Weren’t the Ethiopians and the Lubim a huge army, with chariots and horsemen exceeding many? Yet, because you relied on Yahweh, he delivered them into your hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Yahweh’s eyes run back and forth throughout the whole earth, to show himself strong in the behalf of them whose heart is perfect toward him. You have done foolishly in this; for from now on you will have wars.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Asa was angry with the seer, and put him in the prison; for he was in a rage with him because of this thing. Asa oppressed some of the people at the same time.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, the acts of Asa, first and last, behold, they are written in the book of the kings of Judah and Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the thirty-ninth year of his reign, Asa was diseased in his feet. His disease was exceedingly great: yet in his disease he didn’t seek Yahweh, but just the physicians.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Asa slept with his fathers, and died in the forty-first year of his reign.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They buried him in his own tomb, which he had dug out for himself in David’s city, and laid him in the bed which was filled with sweet odors and various kinds of spices prepared by the perfumers’ art; and they made a very great fire for him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoshaphat his son reigned in his place, and strengthened himself against Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He placed forces in all the fortified cities of Judah, and set garrisons in the land of Judah, and in the cities of Ephraim, which Asa his father had taken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh was with Jehoshaphat, because he walked in the first ways of his father David, and didn’t seek the Baals,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but sought to the God of his father, and walked in his commandments, and not in the ways of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore Yahweh established the kingdom in his hand. All Judah brought tribute to Jehoshaphat, and he had riches and honor in abundance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His heart was lifted up in the ways of Yahweh. Furthermore, he took away the high places and the Asherah poles out of Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also in the third year of his reign he sent his princes, even Ben Hail, Obadiah, Zechariah, Nethanel, and Micaiah, to teach in the cities of Judah;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and with them the Levites, even Shemaiah, Nethaniah, Zebadiah, Asahel, Shemiramoth, Jehonathan, Adonijah, Tobijah, and Tobadonijah, the Levites; and with them Elishama and Jehoram, the priests.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They taught in Judah, having the book of Yahweh’s law with them. They went about throughout all the cities of Judah and taught among the people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The fear of Yahweh fell on all the kingdoms of the lands that were around Judah, so that they made no war against Jehoshaphat.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Some of the Philistines brought Jehoshaphat presents and silver for tribute. The Arabians also brought him flocks, seven thousand seven hundred rams, and seven thousand seven hundred male goats.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoshaphat grew great exceedingly; and he built fortresses and store cities in Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He had many works in the cities of Judah; and men of war, mighty men of valor, in Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This was the numbering of them according to their fathers’ houses: From Judah, the captains of thousands: Adnah the captain, and with him three hundred thousand mighty men of valor;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and next to him Jehohanan the captain, and with him two hundred eighty thousand;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and next to him Amasiah the son of Zichri, who willingly offered himself to Yahweh, and with him two hundred thousand mighty men of valor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: From Benjamin: Eliada, a mighty man of valor, and with him two hundred thousand armed with bow and shield;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and next to him Jehozabad, and with him one hundred eighty thousand ready and prepared for war.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These were those who waited on the king, besides those whom the king put in the fortified cities throughout all Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Jehoshaphat had riches and honor in abundance; and he allied himself with Ahab.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After some years, he went down to Ahab to Samaria. Ahab killed sheep and cattle for him in abundance, and for the people who were with him, and moved him to go up with him to Ramoth Gilead.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ahab king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat king of Judah, “Will you go with me to Ramoth Gilead?” He answered him, “I am as you are, and my people as your people. We will be with you in the war.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoshaphat said to the king of Israel, “Please inquire first for Yahweh’s word.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the king of Israel gathered the prophets together, four hundred men, and said to them, “Shall we go to Ramoth Gilead to battle, or shall I forbear?” They said, “Go up; for God will deliver it into the hand of the king.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jehoshaphat said, “Isn’t there here a prophet of Yahweh besides, that we may inquire of him?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “There is yet one man by whom we may inquire of Yahweh; but I hate him, for he never prophesies good concerning me, but always evil. He is Micaiah the son of Imla.” Jehoshaphat said, “Don’t let the king say so.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the king of Israel called an officer, and said, “Get Micaiah the son of Imla quickly.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat the king of Judah each sat on his throne, arrayed in their robes, and they were sitting in an open place at the entrance of the gate of Samaria; and all the prophets were prophesying before them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Zedekiah the son of Chenaanah made himself horns of iron and said, “Yahweh says, ‘With these you shall push the Syrians, until they are consumed.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the prophets prophesied so, saying, “Go up to Ramoth Gilead, and prosper; for Yahweh will deliver it into the hand of the king.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The messenger who went to call Micaiah spoke to him, saying, “Behold, the words of the prophets declare good to the king with one mouth. Let your word therefore, please be like one of theirs, and speak good.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Micaiah said, “As Yahweh lives, what my God says, that will I speak.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he had come to the king, the king said to him, “Micaiah, shall we go to Ramoth Gilead to battle, or shall I forbear?” He said, “Go up, and prosper. They shall be delivered into your hand.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king said to him, “How many times shall I adjure you that you speak to me nothing but the truth in Yahweh’s name?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “I saw all Israel scattered on the mountains, as sheep that have no shepherd. Yahweh said, ‘These have no master. Let them each return to his house in peace.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “Didn’t I tell you that he would not prophesy good concerning me, but evil?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Micaiah said, “Therefore hear Yahweh’s word: I saw Yahweh sitting on his throne, and all the army of heaven standing on his right hand and on his left.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh said, ‘Who will entice Ahab king of Israel, that he may go up and fall at Ramoth Gilead?’ One spoke saying in this way, and another saying in that way.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A spirit came out, stood before Yahweh, and said, ‘I will entice him.’ “Yahweh said to him, ‘How?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “He said, ‘I will go, and will be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets.’ “He said, ‘You will entice him, and will prevail also. Go and do so.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now therefore, behold, Yahweh has put a lying spirit in the mouth of these your prophets; and Yahweh has spoken evil concerning you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Zedekiah the son of Chenaanah came near, and struck Micaiah on the cheek, and said, “Which way did Yahweh’s Spirit go from me to speak to you?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Micaiah said, “Behold, you shall see on that day, when you go into an inner room to hide yourself.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king of Israel said, “Take Micaiah, and carry him back to Amon the governor of the city, and to Joash the king’s son;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and say, ‘Thus says the king, “Put this fellow in the prison, and feed him with bread of affliction and with water of affliction, until I return in peace.”’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Micaiah said, “If you return at all in peace, Yahweh has not spoken by me.” He said, “Listen, you people, all of you!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat the king of Judah went up to Ramoth Gilead.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “I will disguise myself, and go into the battle; but you put on your robes.” So the king of Israel disguised himself; and they went into the battle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the king of Syria had commanded the captains of his chariots, saying, “Don’t fight with small nor great, except only with the king of Israel.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the captains of the chariots saw Jehoshaphat, they said, “It is the king of Israel!” Therefore they turned around to fight against him. But Jehoshaphat cried out, and Yahweh helped him; and God moved them to depart from him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the captains of the chariots saw that it was not the king of Israel, they turned back from pursuing him.

translations.name: World English Bible
