translations.text: A certain man drew his bow at random, and struck the king of Israel between the joints of the armor. Therefore he said to the driver of the chariot, “Turn your hand, and carry me out of the army; for I am severely wounded.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The battle increased that day. However the king of Israel propped himself up in his chariot against the Syrians until the evening; and at about sunset, he died.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoshaphat the king of Judah returned to his house in peace to Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehu the son of Hanani the seer went out to meet him, and said to king Jehoshaphat, “Should you help the wicked, and love those who hate Yahweh? Because of this, wrath is on you from before Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless there are good things found in you, in that you have put away the Asheroth out of the land, and have set your heart to seek God.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoshaphat lived at Jerusalem; and he went out again among the people from Beersheba to the hill country of Ephraim, and brought them back to Yahweh, the God of their fathers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He set judges in the land throughout all the fortified cities of Judah, city by city,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and said to the judges, “Consider what you do, for you don’t judge for man, but for Yahweh; and he is with you in the judgment.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore let the fear of Yahweh be on you. Take heed and do it; for there is no iniquity with Yahweh our God, nor respect of persons, nor taking of bribes.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover in Jerusalem Jehoshaphat appointed Levites and priests, and of the heads of the fathers’ households of Israel, for the judgment of Yahweh, and for controversies. They returned to Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He commanded them, saying, “You shall do this in the fear of Yahweh, faithfully, and with a perfect heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whenever any controversy comes to you from your brothers who dwell in their cities, between blood and blood, between law and commandment, statutes and ordinances, you must warn them, that they not be guilty towards Yahweh, and so wrath come on you and on your brothers. Do this, and you will not be guilty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, Amariah the chief priest is over you in all matters of Yahweh; and Zebadiah the son of Ishmael, the ruler of the house of Judah, in all the king’s matters. Also the Levites shall be officers before you. Deal courageously, and may Yahweh be with the good.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After this, the children of Moab, the children of Ammon, and with them some of the Ammonites, came against Jehoshaphat to battle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then some came who told Jehoshaphat, saying, “A great multitude is coming against you from beyond the sea from Syria. Behold, they are in Hazazon Tamar” (that is, En Gedi).

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoshaphat was alarmed, and set himself to seek to Yahweh. He proclaimed a fast throughout all Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Judah gathered themselves together to seek help from Yahweh. They came out of all the cities of Judah to seek Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoshaphat stood in the assembly of Judah and Jerusalem, in Yahweh’s house, before the new court;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and he said, “Yahweh, the God of our fathers, aren’t you God in heaven? Aren’t you ruler over all the kingdoms of the nations? Power and might are in your hand, so that no one is able to withstand you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Didn’t you, our God, drive out the inhabitants of this land before your people Israel, and give it to the offspring  of Abraham your friend forever?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They lived in it, and have built you a sanctuary in it for your name, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: ‘If evil comes on us — the sword, judgment, pestilence, or famine — we will stand before this house, and before you (for your name is in this house), and cry to you in our affliction, and you will hear and save.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now, behold, the children of Ammon and Moab and Mount Seir, whom you would not let Israel invade when they came out of the land of Egypt, but they turned aside from them, and didn’t destroy them;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: behold, how they reward us, to come to cast us out of your possession, which you have given us to inherit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Our God, will you not judge them? For we have no might against this great company that comes against us. We don’t know what to do, but our eyes are on you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All Judah stood before Yahweh, with their little ones, their wives, and their children.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Yahweh’s Spirit came on Jahaziel the son of Zechariah, the son of Benaiah, the son of Jeiel, the son of Mattaniah, the Levite, of the sons of Asaph, in the middle of the assembly;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and he said, “Listen, all Judah, and you inhabitants of Jerusalem, and you, king Jehoshaphat. Yahweh says to you, ‘Don’t be afraid, and don’t be dismayed because of this great multitude; for the battle is not yours, but God’s.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Tomorrow, go down against them. Behold, they are coming up by the ascent of Ziz. You will find them at the end of the valley, before the wilderness of Jeruel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You will not need to fight this battle. Set yourselves, stand still, and see the salvation of Yahweh with you, O Judah and Jerusalem. Don’t be afraid, nor be dismayed. Go out against them tomorrow, for Yahweh is with you.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoshaphat bowed his head with his face to the ground; and all Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem fell down before Yahweh, worshiping Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Levites, of the children of the Kohathites and of the children of the Korahites, stood up to praise Yahweh, the God of Israel, with an exceeding loud voice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They rose early in the morning, and went out into the wilderness of Tekoa. As they went out, Jehoshaphat stood and said, “Listen to me, Judah, and you inhabitants of Jerusalem! Believe in Yahweh your God, so you will be established! Believe his prophets, so you will prosper.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he had taken counsel with the people, he appointed those who were to sing to Yahweh, and give praise in holy array, as they go out before the army, and say, “Give thanks to Yahweh; for his loving kindness endures forever.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they began to sing and to praise, Yahweh set ambushers against the children of Ammon, Moab, and Mount Seir, who had come against Judah; and they were struck.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the children of Ammon and Moab stood up against the inhabitants of Mount Seir to utterly kill and destroy them: and when they had finished the inhabitants of Seir, everyone helped to destroy another.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Judah came to the place overlooking the wilderness, they looked at the multitude; and behold, they were dead bodies fallen to the earth, and there were none who escaped.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Jehoshaphat and his people came to take their plunder, they found among them in abundance both riches and dead bodies, and precious jewels, which they stripped off for themselves, more than they could carry away. They took plunder for three days, it was so much.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the fourth day, they assembled themselves in Beracah Valley, for there they blessed Yahweh. Therefore the name of that place was called “Beracah Valley” to this day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then they returned, every man of Judah and Jerusalem, with Jehoshaphat in front of them, to go again to Jerusalem with joy; for Yahweh had made them to rejoice over their enemies.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They came to Jerusalem with stringed instruments, harps, and trumpets to Yahweh’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The fear of God was on all the kingdoms of the countries, when they heard that Yahweh fought against the enemies of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the realm of Jehoshaphat was quiet, for his God gave him rest all around.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoshaphat reigned over Judah. He was thirty-five years old when he began to reign; and he reigned twenty-five years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Azubah the daughter of Shilhi.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He walked in the way of Asa his father, and didn’t turn aside from it, doing that which was right in Yahweh’s eyes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: However the high places were not taken away, and the people had still not set their hearts on the God of their fathers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the rest of the acts of Jehoshaphat, first and last, behold, they are written in the history of Jehu the son of Hanani, which is included in the book of the kings of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After this, Jehoshaphat king of Judah joined himself with Ahaziah king of Israel. The same did very wickedly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He joined himself with him to make ships to go to Tarshish. They made the ships in Ezion Geber.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Eliezer the son of Dodavahu of Mareshah prophesied against Jehoshaphat, saying, “Because you have joined yourself with Ahaziah, Yahweh has destroyed your works.” The ships were wrecked, so that they were not able to go to Tarshish.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoshaphat slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in David’s city, and Jehoram his son reigned in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He had brothers, the sons of Jehoshaphat: Azariah, Jehiel, Zechariah, Azariah, Michael, and Shephatiah. All these were the sons of Jehoshaphat king of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Their father gave them great gifts of silver, of gold, and of precious things, with fortified cities in Judah; but he gave the kingdom to Jehoram, because he was the firstborn.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now when Jehoram had risen up over the kingdom of his father, and had strengthened himself, he killed all his brothers with the sword, and also some of the princes of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoram was thirty-two years old when he began to reign, and he reigned eight years in Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He walked in the way of the kings of Israel, as did Ahab’s house; for he had Ahab’s daughter as his wife. He did that which was evil in Yahweh’s sight.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: However Yahweh would not destroy David’s house, because of the covenant that he had made with David, and as he promised to give a lamp to him and to his children always.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In his days Edom revolted from under the hand of Judah, and made a king over themselves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Jehoram went there with his captains and all his chariots with him. He rose up by night and struck the Edomites who surrounded him, along with the captains of the chariots.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Edom revolted from under the hand of Judah to this day. Then Libnah revolted at the same time from under his hand, because he had forsaken Yahweh, the God of his fathers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover he made high places in the mountains of Judah, and made the inhabitants of Jerusalem play the prostitute, and led Judah astray.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A letter came to him from Elijah the prophet, saying, “Yahweh, the God of David your father, says, ‘Because you have not walked in the ways of Jehoshaphat your father, nor in the ways of Asa king of Judah,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but have walked in the way of the kings of Israel, and have made Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem to play the prostitute like Ahab’s house did, and also have slain your brothers of your father’s house, who were better than yourself,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: behold, Yahweh will strike your people with a great plague, including your children, your wives, and all your posessions;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and you will have great sickness with a disease of your bowels, until your bowels fall out by reason of the sickness, day by day.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh stirred up against Jehoram the spirit of the Philistines, and of the Arabians who are beside the Ethiopians;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and they came up against Judah, broke into it, and carried away all the posessions that were found in the king’s house, including his sons and his wives; so that there was no son left him, except Jehoahaz, the youngest of his sons.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After all this Yahweh struck him in his bowels with an incurable disease.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In process of time, at the end of two years, his bowels fell out by reason of his sickness, and he died of severe diseases. His people made no burning for him, like the burning of his fathers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He was thirty-two years old when he began to reign, and he reigned in Jerusalem eight years. He departed without being missed; and they buried him in David’s city, but not in the tombs of the kings.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The inhabitants of Jerusalem made Ahaziah his youngest son king in his place, because the band of men who came with the Arabians to the camp had slain all the oldest. So Ahaziah the son of Jehoram king of Judah reigned.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ahaziah was forty-two years old when he began to reign, and he reigned one year in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Athaliah the daughter of Omri.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also walked in the ways of Ahab’s house, because his mother was his counselor in acting wickedly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He did that which was evil in Yahweh’s sight, as did Ahab’s house, for they were his counselors after the death of his father, to his destruction.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also followed their counsel, and went with Jehoram the son of Ahab king of Israel to war against Hazael king of Syria at Ramoth Gilead, and the Syrians wounded Joram.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He returned to be healed in Jezreel of the wounds which they had given him at Ramah, when he fought against Hazael king of Syria. Azariah the son of Jehoram king of Judah went down to see Jehoram the son of Ahab in Jezreel, because he was sick.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the destruction of Ahaziah was of God, in that he went to Joram; for when he had come, he went out with Jehoram against Jehu the son of Nimshi, whom Yahweh had anointed to cut off Ahab’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Jehu was executing judgment on Ahab’s house, he found the princes of Judah and the sons of the brothers of Ahaziah, serving Ahaziah, and killed them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He sought Ahaziah, and they caught him (now he was hiding in Samaria), and they brought him to Jehu, and killed him; and they buried him, for they said, “He is the son of Jehoshaphat, who sought Yahweh with all his heart.” The house of Ahaziah had no power to hold the kingdom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now when Athaliah the mother of Ahaziah saw that her son was dead, she arose and destroyed all the royal offspring  of the house of Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jehoshabeath, the king’s daughter, took Joash the son of Ahaziah, and stealthily rescued him from among the king’s sons who were slain, and put him and his nurse in the bedroom. So Jehoshabeath, the daughter of king Jehoram, the wife of Jehoiada the priest (for she was the sister of Ahaziah), hid him from Athaliah, so that she didn’t kill him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He was with them hidden in God’s house six years while Athaliah reigned over the land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the seventh year, Jehoiada strengthened himself, and took the captains of hundreds, Azariah the son of Jeroham, Ishmael the son of Jehohanan, Azariah the son of Obed, Maaseiah the son of Adaiah, and Elishaphat the son of Zichri, into a covenant with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They went around in Judah, and gathered the Levites out of all the cities of Judah, and the heads of fathers’ households of Israel, and they came to Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the assembly made a covenant with the king in God’s house. He said to them, “Behold, the king’s son must reign, as Yahweh has spoken concerning the sons of David.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This is the thing that you must do. A third part of you, who come in on the Sabbath, of the priests and of the Levites, shall be porters of the thresholds.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A third part shall be at the king’s house; and a third part at the gate of the foundation. All the people will be in the courts of Yahweh’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But let no one come into Yahweh’s house, except the priests and those who minister of the Levites. They shall come in, for they are holy, but all the people shall follow Yahweh’s instructions.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Levites shall surround the king, every man with his weapons in his hand. Whoever comes into the house, let him be slain. Be with the king when he comes in, and when he goes out.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the Levites and all Judah did according to all that Jehoiada the priest commanded: and they each took his men, those who were to come in on the Sabbath; with those who were to go out on the Sabbath; for Jehoiada the priest didn’t dismiss the shift.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoiada the priest delivered to the captains of hundreds the spears, and bucklers, and shields, that had been king David’s, which were in God’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He set all the people, every man with his weapon in his hand, from the right side of the house to the left side of the house, near the altar and the house, around the king.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then they brought out the king’s son, and put the crown on him, and gave him the testimony, and made him king. Jehoiada and his sons anointed him, and they said, “Long live the king!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Athaliah heard the noise of the people running and praising the king, she came to the people into Yahweh’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then she looked, and, behold, the king stood by his pillar at the entrance, and the captains and the trumpets by the king. All the people of the land rejoiced, and blew trumpets. The singers also played musical instruments, and led the singing of praise. Then Athaliah tore her clothes, and said, “Treason! treason!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoiada the priest brought out the captains of hundreds who were set over the army, and said to them, “Bring her out between the ranks; and whoever follows her, let him be slain with the sword.” For the priest said, “Don’t kill her in Yahweh’s house.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So they made way for her. She went to the entrance of the horse gate to the king’s house; and they killed her there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoiada made a covenant between himself, all the people, and the king, that they should be Yahweh’s people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the people went to the house of Baal, broke it down, broke his altars and his images in pieces, and killed Mattan the priest of Baal before the altars.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoiada appointed the officers of Yahweh’s house under the hand of the priests the Levites, whom David had distributed in Yahweh’s house, to offer the burnt offerings of Yahweh, as it is written in the law of Moses, with rejoicing and with singing, as David had ordered.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He set the porters at the gates of Yahweh’s house, that no one who was unclean in anything should enter in.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took the captains of hundreds, the nobles, the governors of the people, and all the people of the land, and brought the king down from Yahweh’s house. They came through the upper gate to the king’s house, and set the king on the throne of the kingdom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So all the people of the land rejoiced, and the city was quiet. They had slain Athaliah with the sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joash was seven years old when he began to reign, and he reigned forty years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Zibiah, of Beersheba.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joash did that which was right in Yahweh’s eyes all the days of Jehoiada the priest.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoiada took for him two wives, and he became the father of sons and daughters.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After this, Joash intended to restore Yahweh’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He gathered together the priests and the Levites, and said to them, “Go out to the cities of Judah, and gather money to repair the house of your God from all Israel from year to year. See that you expedite this matter.” However the Levites didn’t do it right away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king called for Jehoiada the chief, and said to him, “Why haven’t you required of the Levites to bring in the tax of Moses the servant of Yahweh, and of the assembly of Israel, out of Judah and out of Jerusalem, for the Tent of the Testimony?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the sons of Athaliah, that wicked woman, had broken up God’s house; and they also gave all the dedicated things of Yahweh’s house to the Baals.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the king commanded, and they made a chest, and set it outside at the gate of Yahweh’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They made a proclamation through Judah and Jerusalem, to bring in for Yahweh the tax that Moses the servant of God laid on Israel in the wilderness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the princes and all the people rejoiced, and brought in, and cast into the chest, until they had filled it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whenever the chest was brought to the king’s officers by the hand of the Levites, and when they saw that there was much money, the king’s scribe and the chief priest’s officer came and emptied the chest, and took it, and carried it to its place again. Thus they did day by day, and gathered money in abundance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king and Jehoiada gave it to those who did the work of the service of Yahweh’s house. They hired masons and carpenters to restore Yahweh’s house, and also those who worked iron and brass to repair Yahweh’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the workmen worked, and the work of repairing went forward in their hands. They set up God’s house as it was designed, and strengthened it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they had finished, they brought the rest of the money before the king and Jehoiada, from which were made vessels for Yahweh’s house, even vessels with which to minister and to offer, including spoons and vessels of gold and silver. They offered burnt offerings in Yahweh’s house continually all the days of Jehoiada.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jehoiada grew old and was full of days, and he died. He was one hundred thirty years old when he died.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They buried him in David’s city among the kings, because he had done good in Israel, and toward God and his house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now after the death of Jehoiada, the princes of Judah came, and bowed down to the king. Then the king listened to them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They abandoned the house of Yahweh, the God of their fathers, and served the Asherah poles and the idols, so wrath came on Judah and Jerusalem for this their guiltiness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yet he sent prophets to them, to bring them again to Yahweh, and they testified against them; but they would not listen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Spirit of God came on Zechariah the son of Jehoiada the priest; and he stood above the people, and said to them, “Thus says God, ‘Why do you disobey Yahweh’s commandments, so that you can’t prosper? Because you have forsaken Yahweh, he has also forsaken you.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They conspired against him, and stoned him with stones at the commandment of the king in the court of Yahweh’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus Joash the king didn’t remember the kindness which Jehoiada his father had done to him, but killed his son. When he died, he said, “May Yahweh look at it, and repay it.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At the end of the year, the army of the Syrians came up against him: and they came to Judah and Jerusalem, and destroyed all the princes of the people from among the people, and sent all their plunder to the king of Damascus.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the army of the Syrians came with a small company of men; and Yahweh delivered a very great army into their hand, because they had forsaken Yahweh, the God of their fathers. So they executed judgment on Joash.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they had departed from him (for they left him very sick), his own servants conspired against him for the blood of the sons of Jehoiada the priest, and killed him on his bed, and he died. They buried him in David’s city, but they didn’t bury him in the tombs of the kings.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These are those who conspired against him: Zabad the son of Shimeath the Ammonitess and Jehozabad the son of Shimrith the Moabitess.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now concerning his sons, the greatness of the burdens laid on him, and the rebuilding of God’s house, behold, they are written in the commentary of the book of the kings. Amaziah his son reigned in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Amaziah was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned twenty-nine years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Jehoaddan, of Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He did that which was right in Yahweh’s eyes, but not with a perfect heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now when the kingdom was established to him, he killed his servants who had killed his father the king.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he didn’t put their children to death, but did according to that which is written in the law in the book of Moses, as Yahweh commanded, saying, “The fathers shall not die for the children, neither shall the children die for the fathers; but every man shall die for his own sin.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover Amaziah gathered Judah together, and ordered them according to their fathers’ houses, under captains of thousands and captains of hundreds, even all Judah and Benjamin. He counted them from twenty years old and upward, and found that there were three hundred thousand chosen men, able to go out to war, who could handle spear and shield.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also hired one hundred thousand mighty men of valor out of Israel for one hundred talents  of silver.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A man of God came to him, saying, “O king, don’t let the army of Israel go with you, for Yahweh is not with Israel, with all the children of Ephraim.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But if you will go, take action, and be strong for the battle. God will overthrow you before the enemy; for God has power to help, and to overthrow.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Amaziah said to the man of God, “But what shall we do for the hundred talents  which I have given to the army of Israel?” The man of God answered, “Yahweh is able to give you much more than this.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Amaziah separated them, the army that had come to him out of Ephraim, to go home again. Therefore their anger was greatly kindled against Judah, and they returned home in fierce anger.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Amaziah took courage, and led his people out, and went to the Valley of Salt, and struck ten thousand of the children of Seir.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Judah carried away ten thousand alive, and brought them to the top of the rock, and threw them down from the top of the rock, so that they all were broken in pieces.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the men of the army whom Amaziah sent back, that they should not go with him to battle, fell on the cities of Judah, from Samaria even to Beth Horon, and struck of them three thousand, and took much plunder.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now after Amaziah had come from the slaughter of the Edomites, he brought the gods of the children of Seir, and set them up to be his gods, and bowed down himself before them, and burned incense to them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore Yahweh’s anger burned against Amaziah, and he sent to him a prophet, who said to him, “Why have you sought after the gods of the people, which have not delivered their own people out of your hand?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As he talked with him, the king said to him, “Have we made you one of the king’s counselors? Stop! Why should you be struck down?” Then the prophet stopped, and said, “I know that God has determined to destroy you, because you have done this, and have not listened to my counsel.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Amaziah king of Judah consulted his advisers, and sent to Joash, the son of Jehoahaz the son of Jehu, king of Israel, saying, “Come, let us look one another in the face.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joash king of Israel sent to Amaziah king of Judah, saying, “The thistle that was in Lebanon sent to the cedar that was in Lebanon, saying, ‘Give your daughter to my son as his wife. Then a wild animal that was in Lebanon passed by, and trampled down the thistle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You say to yourself that you have struck Edom; and your heart lifts you up to boast. Now stay at home. Why should you meddle with trouble, that you should fall, even you, and Judah with you?’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Amaziah would not listen; for it was of God, that he might deliver them into the hand of their enemies, because they had sought after the gods of Edom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Joash king of Israel went up, and he and Amaziah king of Judah looked one another in the face at Beth Shemesh, which belongs to Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Judah was defeated by Israel; and they every man fled to his tent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joash king of Israel took Amaziah king of Judah, the son of Joash the son of Jehoahaz, at Beth Shemesh, and brought him to Jerusalem, and broke down the wall of Jerusalem from the gate of Ephraim to the corner gate, four hundred cubits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took all the gold and silver, and all the vessels that were found in God’s house with Obed-Edom, and the treasures of the king’s house, the hostages also, and returned to Samaria.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Amaziah the son of Joash king of Judah lived for fifteen years after the death of Joash son of Jehoahaz king of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the rest of the acts of Amaziah, first and last, behold, aren’t they written in the book of the kings of Judah and Israel?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now from the time that Amaziah turned away from following Yahweh, they made a conspiracy against him in Jerusalem. He fled to Lachish, but they sent after him to Lachish, and killed him there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They brought him on horses, and buried him with his fathers in the City of Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the people of Judah took Uzziah, who was sixteen years old, and made him king in the place of his father Amaziah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He built Eloth, and restored it to Judah, after that the king slept with his fathers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Uzziah was sixteen years old when he began to reign; and he reigned fifty-two years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Jechiliah, of Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He did that which was right in Yahweh’s eyes, according to all that his father Amaziah had done.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He set himself to seek God in the days of Zechariah, who had understanding in the vision of God; and as long as he sought Yahweh, God made him prosper.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went out and fought against the Philistines, and broke down the wall of Gath, the wall of Jabneh, and the wall of Ashdod; and he built cities in the country of Ashdod, and among the Philistines.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: God helped him against the Philistines, and against the Arabians who lived in Gur Baal, and the Meunim.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Ammonites gave tribute to Uzziah. His name spread abroad even to the entrance of Egypt; for he grew exceedingly strong.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover Uzziah built towers in Jerusalem at the corner gate, at the valley gate, and at the turning of the wall, and fortified them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He built towers in the wilderness, and dug out many cisterns, for he had much livestock; in the lowland also, and in the plain. He had farmers and vineyard keepers in the mountains and in the fruitful fields, for he loved farming.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover Uzziah had an army of fighting men, who went out to war by bands, according to the number of their reckoning made by Jeiel the scribe and Maaseiah the officer, under the hand of Hananiah, one of the king’s captains.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The whole number of the heads of fathers’ households, even the mighty men of valor, was two thousand six hundred.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Under their hand was an army, three hundred seven thousand five hundred, who made war with mighty power, to help the king against the enemy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Uzziah prepared for them, even for all the army, shields, spears, helmets, coats of mail, bows, and stones for slinging.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In Jerusalem, he made devices, invented by skillful men, to be on the towers and on the battlements, with which to shoot arrows and great stones. His name spread far abroad, because he was marvelously helped until he was strong.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when he was strong, his heart was lifted up, so that he did corruptly, and he trespassed against Yahweh his God; for he went into Yahweh’s temple to burn incense on the altar of incense.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Azariah the priest went in after him, and with him eighty priests of Yahweh, who were valiant men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They resisted Uzziah the king, and said to him, “It isn’t for you, Uzziah, to burn incense to Yahweh, but for the priests the sons of Aaron, who are consecrated to burn incense. Go out of the sanctuary, for you have trespassed. It will not be for your honor from Yahweh God.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Uzziah was angry. He had a censer in his hand to burn incense, and while he was angry with the priests, the leprosy broke out on his forehead before the priests in Yahweh’s house, beside the altar of incense.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Azariah the chief priest, and all the priests, looked at him, and behold, he was leprous in his forehead, and they thrust him out quickly from there. Yes, he himself also hurried to go out, because Yahweh had struck him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Uzziah the king was a leper to the day of his death, and lived in a separate house, being a leper; for he was cut off from Yahweh’s house. Jotham his son was over the king’s house, judging the people of the land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the rest of the acts of Uzziah, first and last, Isaiah the prophet, the son of Amoz, wrote.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Uzziah slept with his fathers; and they buried him with his fathers in the field of burial which belonged to the kings, for they said, “He is a leper.” Jotham his son reigned in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jotham was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Jerushah the daughter of Zadok.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He did that which was right in Yahweh’s eyes, according to all that his father Uzziah had done. However he didn’t enter into Yahweh’s temple. The people still acted corruptly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He built the upper gate of Yahweh’s house, and he built much on the wall of Ophel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover he built cities in the hill country of Judah, and in the forests he built fortresses and towers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also fought with the king of the children of Ammon, and prevailed against them. The children of Ammon gave him the same year one hundred talents  of silver, ten thousand cors  of wheat, and ten thousand cors of barley.  The children of Ammon also gave that much to him in the second year, and in the third.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Jotham became mighty, because he ordered his ways before Yahweh his God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the rest of the acts of Jotham, and all his wars, and his ways, behold, they are written in the book of the kings of Israel and Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He was five and twenty years old when he began to reign, and reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jotham slept with his fathers, and they buried him in David’s city; and Ahaz his son reigned in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ahaz was twenty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem. He didn’t do that which was right in Yahweh’s eyes, like David his father,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but he walked in the ways of the kings of Israel, and also made molten images for the Baals.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover he burned incense in the valley of the son of Hinnom, and burned his children in the fire, according to the abominations of the nations whom Yahweh cast out before the children of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He sacrificed and burned incense in the high places, and on the hills, and under every green tree.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore Yahweh his God delivered him into the hand of the king of Syria. They struck him, and carried away from him a great multitude of captives, and brought them to Damascus. He was also delivered into the hand of the king of Israel, who struck him with a great slaughter.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Pekah the son of Remaliah killed in Judah one hundred twenty thousand in one day, all of them valiant men, because they had forsaken Yahweh, the God of their fathers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Zichri, a mighty man of Ephraim, killed Maaseiah the king’s son, Azrikam the ruler of the house, and Elkanah who was next to the king.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Israel carried away captive of their brothers two hundred thousand, women, sons, and daughters, and took also away much plunder from them, and brought the plunder to Samaria.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But a prophet of Yahweh was there, whose name was Oded; and he went out to meet the army that came to Samaria, and said to them, “Behold, because Yahweh, the God of your fathers, was angry with Judah, he has delivered them into your hand, and you have slain them in a rage which has reached up to heaven.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now you intend to degrade the children of Judah and Jerusalem as male and female slaves for yourselves. Aren’t there even with you trespasses of your own against Yahweh your God?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now hear me therefore, and send back the captives that you have taken captive from your brothers, for the fierce wrath of Yahweh is on you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then some of the heads of the children of Ephraim, Azariah the son of Johanan, Berechiah the son of Meshillemoth, Jehizkiah the son of Shallum, and Amasa the son of Hadlai, stood up against those who came from the war,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and said to them, “You must not bring in the captives here, for you intend that which will bring on us a trespass against Yahweh, to add to our sins and to our guilt; for our gulit is great, and there is fierce wrath against Israel.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the armed men left the captives and the plunder before the princes and all the assembly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The men who have been mentioned by name rose up and took the captives, and with the plunder clothed all who were naked among them, dressed them, gave them sandals, and gave them something to eat and to drink, anointed them, carried all the feeble of them on donkeys, and brought them to Jericho, the city of palm trees, to their brothers. Then they returned to Samaria.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At that time king Ahaz sent to the kings of Assyria to help him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For again the Edomites had come and struck Judah, and carried away captives.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Philistines also had invaded the cities of the lowland, and of the South of Judah, and had taken Beth Shemesh, Aijalon, Gederoth, Soco with its villages, Timnah with its villages, and also Gimzo and its villages; and they lived there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Yahweh brought Judah low because of Ahaz king of Israel, because he acted without restraint in Judah and trespassed severely against Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Tilgath Pilneser king of Assyria came to him, and gave him trouble, but didn’t strengthen him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Ahaz took away a portion out of Yahweh’s house, and out of the house of the king and of the princes, and gave it to the king of Assyria; but it didn’t help him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the time of his distress, he trespassed yet more against Yahweh, this same king Ahaz.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he sacrificed to the gods of Damascus, which struck him. He said, “Because the gods of the kings of Syria helped them, so I will sacrifice to them, that they may help me.” But they were the ruin of him, and of all Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ahaz gathered together the vessels of God’s house, and cut the vessels of God’s house in pieces, and shut up the doors of Yahweh’s house; and he made himself altars in every corner of Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In every city of Judah he made high places to burn incense to other gods, and provoked Yahweh, the God of his fathers, to anger.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the rest of his acts, and all his ways, first and last, behold, they are written in the book of the kings of Judah and Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ahaz slept with his fathers, and they buried him in the city, even in Jerusalem, because they didn’t bring him into the tombs of the kings of Israel; and Hezekiah his son reigned in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hezekiah began to reign when he was twenty-five years old, and he reigned twenty-nine years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Abijah, the daughter of Zechariah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He did that which was right in Yahweh’s eyes, according to all that David his father had done.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the first year of his reign, in the first month, he opened the doors of Yahweh’s house, and repaired them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He brought in the priests and the Levites, and gathered them together into the wide place on the east,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and said to them, “Listen to me, you Levites! Now sanctify yourselves, and sanctify Yahweh, the God of your fathers’ house, and carry the filthiness out of the holy place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For our fathers were unfaithful, and have done that which was evil in Yahweh our God’s sight, and have forsaken him, and have turned away their faces from the habitation of Yahweh, and turned their backs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also they have shut up the doors of the porch, and put out the lamps, and have not burned incense nor offered burnt offerings in the holy place to the God of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore Yahweh’s wrath was on Judah and Jerusalem, and he has delivered them to be tossed back and forth, to be an astonishment, and a hissing, as you see with your eyes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For, behold, our fathers have fallen by the sword, and our sons and our daughters and our wives are in captivity for this.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now it is in my heart to make a covenant with Yahweh, the God of Israel, that his fierce anger may turn away from us.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My sons, don’t be negligent now; for Yahweh has chosen you to stand before him, to minister to him, and that you should be his ministers, and burn incense.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the Levites arose, Mahath, the son of Amasai, and Joel the son of Azariah, of the sons of the Kohathites; and of the sons of Merari, Kish the son of Abdi, and Azariah the son of Jehallelel; and of the Gershonites, Joah the son of Zimmah, and Eden the son of Joah;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and of the sons of Elizaphan, Shimri and Jeuel; and of the sons of Asaph, Zechariah and Mattaniah;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and of the sons of Heman, Jehuel and Shimei; and of the sons of Jeduthun, Shemaiah and Uzziel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They gathered their brothers, sanctified themselves, and went in, according to the commandment of the king by Yahweh’s words, to cleanse Yahweh’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The priests went into the inner part of Yahweh’s house to cleanse it, and brought out all the uncleanness that they found in Yahweh’s temple into the court of Yahweh’s house. The Levites took it from there to carry it out to the brook Kidron.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now they began on the first day of the first month to sanctify, and on the eighth day of the month they came to Yahweh’s porch. They sanctified Yahweh’s house in eight days, and on the sixteenth day of the first month they finished.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then they went in to Hezekiah the king within the palace, and said, “We have cleansed all Yahweh’s house, including the altar of burnt offering with all its vessels, and the table of show bread with all its vessels.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover all the vessels, which king Ahaz in his reign threw away when he was unfaithful, have we prepared and sanctified. Behold, they are before Yahweh’s altar.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Hezekiah the king arose early, gathered the princes of the city, and went up to Yahweh’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They brought seven bulls, seven rams, seven lambs, and seven male goats, for a sin offering for the kingdom, for the sanctuary, and for Judah. He commanded the priests the sons of Aaron to offer them on Yahweh’s altar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So they killed the bulls, and the priests received the blood, and sprinkled it on the altar. They killed the rams, and sprinkled the blood on the altar. They also killed the lambs, and sprinkled the blood on the altar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They brought near the male goats for the sin offering before the king and the assembly; and they laid their hands on them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the priests killed them, and they made a sin offering with their blood on the altar, to make atonement for all Israel; for the king commanded that the burnt offering and the sin offering should be made for all Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He set the Levites in Yahweh’s house with cymbals, with stringed instruments, and with harps, according to the commandment of David, of Gad the king’s seer, and Nathan the prophet; for the commandment was from Yahweh by his prophets.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Levites stood with David’s instruments, and the priests with the trumpets.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hezekiah commanded them to offer the burnt offering on the altar. When the burnt offering began, Yahweh’s song also began, along with the trumpets and David king of Israel’s instruments.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the assembly worshiped, the singers sang, and the trumpeters sounded. All this continued until the burnt offering was finished.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they had finished offering, the king and all who were present with him bowed themselves and worshiped.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover Hezekiah the king and the princes commanded the Levites to sing praises to Yahweh with the words of David, and of Asaph the seer. They sang praises with gladness, and they bowed their heads and worshiped.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Hezekiah answered, “Now you have consecrated yourselves to Yahweh. Come near and bring sacrifices and thank offerings into Yahweh’s house.” The assembly brought in sacrifices and thank offerings, and as many as were of a willing heart brought burnt offerings.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The number of the burnt offerings which the assembly brought was seventy bulls, one hundred rams, and two hundred lambs. All these were for a burnt offering to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The consecrated things were six hundred head of cattle and three thousand sheep.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the priests were too few, so that they could not flay all the burnt offerings. Therefore their brothers the Levites helped them, until the work was ended, and until the priests had sanctified themselves; for the Levites were more upright in heart to sanctify themselves than the priests.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also the burnt offerings were in abundance, with the fat of the peace offerings, and with the drink offerings for every burnt offering. So the service of Yahweh’s house was set in order.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hezekiah and all the people rejoiced, because of that which God had prepared for the people; for the thing was done suddenly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hezekiah sent to all Israel and Judah, and wrote letters also to Ephraim and Manasseh, that they should come to Yahweh’s house at Jerusalem, to keep the Passover to Yahweh, the God of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the king had taken counsel with his princes and all the assembly in Jerusalem to keep the Passover in the second month.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For they could not keep it at that time, because the priests had not sanctified themselves in sufficient number, and the people had not gathered themselves together to Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The thing was right in the eyes of the king and of all the assembly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So they established a decree to make proclamation throughout all Israel, from Beersheba even to Dan, that they should come to keep the Passover to Yahweh, the God of Israel, at Jerusalem, for they had not kept it in great numbers in the way it is written.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the couriers went with the letters from the king and his princes throughout all Israel and Judah, and according to the commandment of the king, saying, “You children of Israel, turn again to Yahweh, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, that he may return to the remnant of you that have escaped out of the hand of the kings of Assyria.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t be like your fathers and like your brothers, who trespassed against Yahweh, the God of their fathers, so that he gave them up to desolation, as you see.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now don’t be stiff-necked, as your fathers were, but yield yourselves to Yahweh, and enter into his sanctuary, which he has sanctified forever, and serve Yahweh your God, that his fierce anger may turn away from you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For if you turn again to Yahweh, your brothers and your children will find compassion before those who led them captive, and will come again into this land, because Yahweh your God is gracious and merciful, and will not turn away his face from you, if you return to him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the couriers passed from city to city through the country of Ephraim and Manasseh, even to Zebulun, but people ridiculed them and mocked them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless some men of Asher and Manasseh and of Zebulun humbled themselves, and came to Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also the hand of God came on Judah to give them one heart, to do the commandment of the king and of the princes by Yahweh’s word.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Many people assembled at Jerusalem to keep the feast of unleavened bread in the second month, a very great assembly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They arose and took away the altars that were in Jerusalem, and they took away all the altars for incense and threw them into the brook Kidron.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then they killed the Passover on the fourteenth day of the second month. The priests and the Levites were ashamed, and sanctified themselves, and brought burnt offerings into Yahweh’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They stood in their place after their order, according to the law of Moses the man of God. The priests sprinkled the blood which they received of the hand of the Levites.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For there were many in the assembly who had not sanctified themselves: therefore the Levites were in charge of killing the Passovers for everyone who was not clean, to sanctify them to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For a multitude of the people, even many of Ephraim, Manasseh, Issachar, and Zebulun, had not cleansed themselves, yet they ate the Passover other than the way it is written. For Hezekiah had prayed for them, saying, “May the good Yahweh pardon everyone

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who sets his heart to seek God, Yahweh, the God of his fathers, even if they aren’t clean according to the purification of the sanctuary.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh listened to Hezekiah, and healed the people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Israel who were present at Jerusalem kept the feast of unleavened bread seven days with great gladness. The Levites and the priests praised Yahweh day by day, singing with loud instruments to Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hezekiah spoke encouragingly to all the Levites who had good understanding in the service of Yahweh. So they ate throughout the feast for the seven days, offering sacrifices of peace offerings, and making confession to Yahweh, the God of their fathers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The whole assembly took counsel to keep another seven days, and they kept another seven days with gladness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Hezekiah king of Judah gave to the assembly for offerings one thousand bulls and seven thousand sheep; and the princes gave to the assembly a thousand bulls and ten thousand sheep: and a great number of priests sanctified themselves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the assembly of Judah, with the priests and the Levites, and all the assembly who came out of Israel, and the foreigners who came out of the land of Israel, and who lived in Judah, rejoiced.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So there was great joy in Jerusalem; for since the time of Solomon the son of David king of Israel there was nothing like this in Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the priests the Levites arose and blessed the people. Their voice was heard, and their prayer came up to his holy habitation, even to heaven.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now when all this was finished, all Israel who were present went out to the cities of Judah, and broke the pillars in pieces, cut down the Asherah poles, and broke down the high places and the altars out of all Judah and Benjamin, also in Ephraim and Manasseh, until they had destroyed them all. Then all the children of Israel returned, every man to his possession, into their own cities.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hezekiah appointed the divisions of the priests and the Levites after their divisions, every man according to his service, both the priests and the Levites, for burnt offerings and for peace offerings, to minister, to give thanks, and to praise in the gates of Yahweh’s camp.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also appointed the king’s portion of his posessions for the burnt offerings, for the morning and evening burnt offerings, and the burnt offerings for the Sabbaths, for the new moons, and for the set feasts, as it is written in Yahweh’s law.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover he commanded the people who lived in Jerusalem to give the portion of the priests and the Levites, that they might give themselves to Yahweh’s law.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As soon as the commandment went out, the children of Israel gave in abundance the first fruits of grain, new wine, oil, honey, and of all the increase of the field; and they brought in the tithe of all things abundantly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Israel and Judah, who lived in the cities of Judah, also brought in the tithe of cattle and sheep, and the tithe of dedicated things which were consecrated to Yahweh their God, and laid them in heaps.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the third month they began to lay the foundation of the heaps, and finished them in the seventh month.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Hezekiah and the princes came and saw the heaps, they blessed Yahweh and his people Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Hezekiah questioned the priests and the Levites about the heaps.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Azariah the chief priest, of the house of Zadok, answered him and said, “Since people began to bring the offerings into Yahweh’s house, we have eaten and had enough, and have plenty left over, for Yahweh has blessed his people; and that which is left is this great store.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Hezekiah commanded them to prepare rooms in Yahweh’s house, and they prepared them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They brought in the offerings, the tithes, and the dedicated things faithfully. Conaniah the Levite was ruler over them, and Shimei his brother was second.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehiel, Azaziah, Nahath, Asahel, Jerimoth, Jozabad, Eliel, Ismachiah, Mahath, and Benaiah were overseers under the hand of Conaniah and Shimei his brother, by the appointment of Hezekiah the king and Azariah the ruler of God’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Kore the son of Imnah the Levite, the porter at the east gate, was over the freewill offerings of God, to distribute Yahweh’s offerings and the most holy things.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Under him were Eden, Miniamin, Jeshua, Shemaiah, Amariah, and Shecaniah, in the cities of the priests, in their office of trust, to give to their brothers by divisions, to the great as well as to the small;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: besides those who were listed by genealogy of males, from three years old and upward, even everyone who entered into Yahweh’s house, as the duty of every day required, for their service in their offices according to their divisions;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and those who were listed by genealogy of the priests by their fathers’ houses, and the Levites from twenty years old and upward, in their offices by their divisions;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and those who were listed by genealogy of all their little ones, their wives, their sons, and their daughters, through all the congregation; for in their office of trust they sanctified themselves in holiness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also for the sons of Aaron the priests, who were in the fields of the suburbs of their cities, in every city, there were men who were mentioned by name, to give portions to all the males among the priests, and to all who were listed by genealogy among the Levites.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hezekiah did so throughout all Judah; and he did that which was good, right, and faithful before Yahweh his God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In every work that he began in the service of God’s house, in the law, and in the commandments, to seek his God, he did it with all his heart, and prospered.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After these things and this faithfulness, Sennacherib king of Assyria came, entered into Judah, and encamped against the fortified cities, and intended to win them for himself.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Hezekiah saw that Sennacherib had come, and that he was planning to fight against Jerusalem,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: he took counsel with his princes and his mighty men to stop the waters of the springs which were outside of the city, and they helped him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So, many people gathered together and they stopped all the springs and the brook that flowed through the middle of the land, saying, “Why should the kings of Assyria come, and find abundant water?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took courage, built up all the wall that was broken down, and raised it up to the towers, with the other wall outside, and strengthened Millo in David’s city, and made weapons and shields in abundance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He set captains of war over the people, and gathered them together to him in the wide place at the gate of the city, and spoke encouragingly to them, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Be strong and courageous. Don’t be afraid or dismayed because of the king of Assyria, nor for all the multitude who is with him; for there is a greater one with us than with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: An arm of flesh is with him, but Yahweh our God is with us to help us and to fight our battles.” The people rested themselves on the words of Hezekiah king of Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After this, Sennacherib king of Assyria sent his servants to Jerusalem, (now he was before Lachish, and all his power with him), to Hezekiah king of Judah, and to all Judah who were at Jerusalem, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus says Sennacherib king of Assyria, “In whom do you trust, that you remain under siege in Jerusalem?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Doesn’t Hezekiah persuade you, to give you over to die by famine and by thirst, saying, ‘Yahweh our God will deliver us out of the hand of the king of Assyria?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hasn’t the same Hezekiah taken away his high places and his altars, and commanded Judah and Jerusalem, saying, ‘You shall worship before one altar, and you shall burn incense on it?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t you know what I and my fathers have done to all the peoples of the lands? Were the gods of the nations of the lands in any way able to deliver their land out of my hand?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Who was there among all the gods of those nations which my fathers utterly destroyed, that could deliver his people out of my hand, that your God should be able to deliver you out of my hand?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now therefore don’t let Hezekiah deceive you, nor persuade you in this way. Don’t believe him, for no god of any nation or kingdom was able to deliver his people out of my hand, and out of the hand of my fathers. How much less will your God deliver you out of my hand?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His servants spoke yet more against Yahweh God, and against his servant Hezekiah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also wrote letters insulting Yahweh, the God of Israel, and speaking against him, saying, “As the gods of the nations of the lands, which have not delivered their people out of my hand, so shall the God of Hezekiah not deliver his people out of my hand.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They called out with a loud voice in the Jews’ language to the people of Jerusalem who were on the wall, to frighten them, and to trouble them; that they might take the city.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They spoke of the God of Jerusalem as of the gods of the peoples of the earth, which are the work of men’s hands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hezekiah the king and Isaiah the prophet the son of Amoz, prayed because of this, and cried to heaven.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh sent an angel, who cut off all the mighty men of valor, and the leaders and captains, in the camp of the king of Assyria. So he returned with shame of face to his own land. When he had come into the house of his god, those who came out of his own bowels  killed him there with the sword.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Thus Yahweh saved Hezekiah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem from the hand of Sennacherib the king of Assyria and from the hand of all others, and guided them on every side.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Many brought gifts to Yahweh to Jerusalem, and precious things to Hezekiah king of Judah; so that he was exalted in the sight of all nations from then on.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In those days Hezekiah was terminally ill, and he prayed to Yahweh; and he spoke to him, and gave him a sign.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Hezekiah didn’t reciprocate appropriate to the benefit done for him, because his heart was lifted up. Therefore there was wrath on him, and on Judah and Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Notwithstanding Hezekiah humbled himself for the pride of his heart, both he and the inhabitants of Jerusalem, so that Yahweh’s wrath didn’t come on them in the days of Hezekiah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hezekiah had exceedingly much riches and honor. He provided himself with treasuries for silver, for gold, for precious stones, for spices, for shields, and for all kinds of valuable vessels;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: also storehouses for the increase of grain, new wine, and oil; and stalls for all kinds of animals, and flocks in folds.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover he provided for himself cities, and possessions of flocks and herds in abundance; for God had given him abundant posessions.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This same Hezekiah also stopped the upper spring of the waters of Gihon, and brought them straight down on the west side of David’s city. Hezekiah prospered in all his works.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: However concerning the ambassadors of the princes of Babylon, who sent to him to inquire of the wonder that was done in the land, God left him, to try him, that he might know all that was in his heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the rest of the acts of Hezekiah, and his good deeds, behold, they are written in the vision of Isaiah the prophet the son of Amoz, in the book of the kings of Judah and Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hezekiah slept with his fathers, and they buried him in the ascent of the tombs of the sons of David. All Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem honored him at his death. Manasseh his son reigned in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Manasseh was twelve years old when he began to reign, and he reigned fifty-five years in Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He did that which was evil in Yahweh’s sight, after the abominations of the nations whom Yahweh cast out before the children of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he built again the high places which Hezekiah his father had broken down; and he raised up altars for the Baals, made Asheroth, and worshiped all the army of the sky, and served them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He built altars in Yahweh’s house, of which Yahweh said, “My name shall be in Jerusalem forever.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He built altars for all the army of the sky in the two courts of Yahweh’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also made his children to pass through the fire in the valley of the son of Hinnom. He practiced sorcery, divination, and witchcraft, and dealt with those who had familiar spirits, and with wizards. He did much evil in Yahweh’s sight, to provoke him to anger.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He set the engraved image of the idol, which he had made, in God’s house, of which God said to David and to Solomon his son, “In this house, and in Jerusalem, which I have chosen out of all the tribes of Israel, will I put my name forever.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will not any more remove the foot of Israel from off the land which I have appointed for your fathers, if only they will observe to do all that I have commanded them, even all the law, the statutes, and the ordinances given by Moses.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Manasseh seduced Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem, so that they did more evil than the nations whom Yahweh destroyed before the children of Israel did.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh spoke to Manasseh, and to his people; but they didn’t listen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore Yahweh brought on them the captains of the army of the king of Assyria, who took Manasseh in chains, bound him with fetters, and carried him to Babylon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he was in distress, he begged Yahweh his God, and humbled himself greatly before the God of his fathers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He prayed to him; and he was entreated by him, and heard his supplication, and brought him again to Jerusalem into his kingdom. Then Manasseh knew that Yahweh was God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now after this, he built an outer wall to David’s city, on the west side of Gihon, in the valley, even to the entrance at the fish gate. He encircled Ophel with it, and raised it up to a very great height; and he put valiant captains in all the fortified cities of Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took away the foreign gods, and the idol out of Yahweh’s house, and all the altars that he had built in the mountain of Yahweh’s house, and in Jerusalem, and cast them out of the city.

translations.name: World English Bible
