translations.text: He built up Yahweh’s altar, and offered sacrifices of peace offerings and of thanksgiving on it, and commanded Judah to serve Yahweh, the God of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless the people sacrificed still in the high places, but only to Yahweh their God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the rest of the acts of Manasseh, and his prayer to his God, and the words of the seers who spoke to him in the name of Yahweh, the God of Israel, behold, they are written among the acts of the kings of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His prayer also, and how God was entreated of him, and all his sin and his trespass, and the places in which he built high places, and set up the Asherah poles and the engraved images, before he humbled himself: behold, they are written in the history of Hozai.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Manasseh slept with his fathers, and they buried him in his own house; and Amon his son reigned in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Amon was twenty-two years old when he began to reign; and he reigned two years in Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He did that which was evil in Yahweh’s sight, as did Manasseh his father; and Amon sacrificed to all the engraved images which Manasseh his father had made, and served them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He didn’t humble himself before Yahweh, as Manasseh his father had humbled himself; but this same Amon trespassed more and more.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His servants conspired against him, and put him to death in his own house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the people of the land killed all those who had conspired against king Amon; and the people of the land made Josiah his son king in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Josiah was eight years old when he began to reign, and he reigned thirty-one years in Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He did that which was right in Yahweh’s eyes, and walked in the ways of David his father, and didn’t turn aside to the right hand or to the left.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For in the eighth year of his reign, while he was yet young, he began to seek after the God of David his father; and in the twelfth year he began to purge Judah and Jerusalem from the high places, the Asherah poles, the engraved images, and the molten images.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They broke down the altars of the Baals in his presence; and he cut down the incense altars that were on high above them. He broke the Asherah poles, the engraved images, and the molten images in pieces, made dust of them, and scattered it on the graves of those who had sacrificed to them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He burned the bones of the priests on their altars, and purged Judah and Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He did this in the cities of Manasseh, Ephraim, and Simeon, even to Naphtali, around in their ruins.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He broke down the altars, and beat the Asherah poles and the engraved images into powder, and cut down all the incense altars throughout all the land of Israel, then returned to Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now in the eighteenth year of his reign, when he had purged the land and the house, he sent Shaphan the son of Azaliah, and Maaseiah the governor of the city, and Joah the son of Joahaz the recorder, to repair Yahweh his God’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They came to Hilkiah the high priest, and delivered the money that was brought into God’s house, which the Levites, the keepers of the threshold, had gathered from the hands of Manasseh, Ephraim, of all the remnant of Israel, of all Judah and Benjamin, and of the inhabitants of Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They delivered it into the hands of the workmen who had the oversight of Yahweh’s house; and the workmen who labored in Yahweh’s house gave it to mend and repair the house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They gave it to the carpenters and to the builders, to buy cut stone and timber for couplings, and to make beams for the houses which the kings of Judah had destroyed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The men did the work faithfully. Their overseers were Jahath and Obadiah, the Levites, of the sons of Merari; and Zechariah and Meshullam, of the sons of the Kohathites, to give direction; and others of the Levites, who were all skillful with musical instruments.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Also they were over the bearers of burdens, and directed all who did the work in every kind of service. Of the Levites, there were scribes, officials, and porters.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they brought out the money that was brought into Yahweh’s house, Hilkiah the priest found the book of Yahweh’s law given by Moses.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hilkiah answered Shaphan the scribe, “I have found the book of the law in Yahweh’s house.” So Hilkiah delivered the book to Shaphan.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Shaphan carried the book to the king, and moreover brought back word to the king, saying, “All that was committed to your servants, they are doing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They have emptied out the money that was found in Yahweh’s house, and have delivered it into the hand of the overseers, and into the hand of the workmen.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Shaphan the scribe told the king, saying, “Hilkiah the priest has delivered me a book.” Shaphan read from it to the king.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the king had heard the words of the law, he tore his clothes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king commanded Hilkiah, Ahikam the son of Shaphan, Abdon the son of Micah, Shaphan the scribe, and Asaiah the king’s servant, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Go inquire of Yahweh for me, and for those who are left in Israel and in Judah, concerning the words of the book that is found; for great is Yahweh’s wrath that is poured out on us, because our fathers have not kept Yahweh’s word, to do according to all that is written in this book.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So Hilkiah, and they whom the king had commanded, went to Huldah the prophetess, the wife of Shallum the son of Tokhath, the son of Hasrah, keeper of the wardrobe (now she lived in Jerusalem in the second quarter), and they spoke to her to that effect.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She said to them, “Yahweh, the God of Israel says: ‘Tell the man who sent you to me,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Yahweh says, ‘Behold, I will bring evil on this place, and on its inhabitants, even all the curses that are written in the book which they have read before the king of Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Because they have forsaken me, and have burned incense to other gods, that they might provoke me to anger with all the works of their hands; therefore my wrath is poured out on this place, and it will not be quenched.’”’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But to the king of Judah, who sent you to inquire of Yahweh, you shall tell him this, ‘Yahweh, the God of Israel says: “About the words which you have heard,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: because your heart was tender, and you humbled yourself before God, when you heard his words against this place, and against its inhabitants, and have humbled yourself before me, and have torn your clothes, and wept before me, I also have heard you,” says Yahweh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Behold, I will gather you to your fathers, and you will be gathered to your grave in peace. Your eyes won’t see all the evil that I will bring on this place and on its inhabitants.”’” They brought back word to the king.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the king sent and gathered together all the elders of Judah and Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king went up to Yahweh’s house, with all the men of Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem, the priests, the Levites, and all the people, both great and small; and he read in their hearing all the words of the book of the covenant that was found in Yahweh’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king stood in his place, and made a covenant before Yahweh, to walk after Yahweh, and to keep his commandments, and his testimonies, and his statutes, with all his heart, and with all his soul, to perform the words of the covenant that were written in this book.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He caused all who were found in Jerusalem and Benjamin to stand. The inhabitants of Jerusalem did according to the covenant of God, the God of their fathers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Josiah took away all the abominations out of all the countries that belonged to the children of Israel, and made all who were found in Israel to serve, even to serve Yahweh their God. All his days they didn’t depart from following Yahweh, the God of their fathers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Josiah kept a Passover to Yahweh in Jerusalem. They killed the Passover on the fourteenth day of the first month.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He set the priests in their offices, and encouraged them in the service of Yahweh’s house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to the Levites who taught all Israel, who were holy to Yahweh, “Put the holy ark in the house which Solomon the son of David king of Israel built. It will no longer be a burden on your shoulders. Now serve Yahweh your God, and his people Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Prepare yourselves after your fathers’ houses by your divisions, according to the writing of David king of Israel, and according to the writing of Solomon his son.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Stand in the holy place according to the divisions of the fathers’ houses of your brothers the children of the people, and let there be for each a portion of a fathers’ house of the Levites.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Kill the Passover, sanctify yourselves, and prepare for your brothers, to do according to Yahweh’s word by Moses.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Josiah gave to the children of the people, of the flock, lambs and young goats, all of them for the Passover offerings, to all who were present, to the number of thirty thousand, and three thousand bulls. These were of the king’s substance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His princes gave for a freewill offering to the people, to the priests, and to the Levites. Hilkiah, Zechariah, and Jehiel, the rulers of God’s house, gave to the priests for the Passover offerings two thousand six hundred small livestock, and three hundred head of cattle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Conaniah also, and Shemaiah and Nethanel, his brothers, and Hashabiah, Jeiel, and Jozabad, the chiefs of the Levites, gave to the Levites for the Passover offerings five thousand small livestock and five hundred head of cattle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So the service was prepared, and the priests stood in their place, and the Levites by their divisions, according to the king’s commandment.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They killed the Passover, and the priests sprinkled the blood which they received of their hand, and the Levites flayed them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They removed the burnt offerings, that they might give them according to the divisions of the fathers’ houses of the children of the people, to offer to Yahweh, as it is written in the book of Moses. They did the same with the cattle.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They roasted the Passover with fire according to the ordinance. The boiled the holy offerings in pots, in cauldrons, and in pans, and carried them quickly to all the children of the people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Afterward they prepared for themselves and for the priests, because the priests the sons of Aaron were busy with offering the burnt offerings and the fat until night. Therefore the Levites prepared for themselves and for the priests the sons of Aaron.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The singers the sons of Asaph were in their place, according to the commandment of David, Asaph, Heman, and Jeduthun the king’s seer; and the porters were at every gate. They didn’t need to depart from their service, because their brothers the Levites prepared for them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So all the service of Yahweh was prepared the same day, to keep the Passover, and to offer burnt offerings on Yahweh’s altar, according to the commandment of king Josiah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The children of Israel who were present kept the Passover at that time, and the feast of unleavened bread seven days.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There was no Passover like that kept in Israel from the days of Samuel the prophet, nor did any of the kings of Israel keep such a Passover as Josiah kept, with the priests, the Levites, and all Judah and Israel who were present, and the inhabitants of Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This passover was kept in the eighteenth year of the reign of Josiah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After all this, when Josiah had prepared the temple, Neco king of Egypt went up to fight against Carchemish by the Euphrates, and Josiah went out against him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he sent ambassadors to him, saying, “What have I to do with you, you king of Judah? I come not against you today, but against the house with which I have war. God has commanded me to make haste. Beware that it is God who is with me, that he not destroy you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless Josiah would not turn his face from him, but disguised himself, that he might fight with him, and didn’t listen to the words of Neco from the mouth of God, and came to fight in the valley of Megiddo.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The archers shot at king Josiah; and the king said to his servants, “Take me away, because I am seriously wounded!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So his servants took him out of the chariot, and put him in the second chariot that he had, and brought him to Jerusalem; and he died, and was buried in the tombs of his fathers. All Judah and Jerusalem mourned for Josiah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jeremiah lamented for Josiah, and all the singing men and singing women spoke of Josiah in their lamentations to this day; and they made them an ordinance in Israel. Behold, they are written in the lamentations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the rest of the acts of Josiah, and his good deeds, according to that which is written in Yahweh’s law,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and his acts, first and last, behold, they are written in the book of the kings of Israel and Judah.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the people of the land took Jehoahaz the son of Josiah, and made him king in his father’s place in Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joahaz was twenty-three years old when he began to reign; and he reigned three months in Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king of Egypt removed him from office at Jerusalem, and fined the land one hundred talents of silver and a talent  of gold.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king of Egypt made Eliakim his brother king over Judah and Jerusalem, and changed his name to Jehoiakim. Neco took Joahaz his brother, and carried him to Egypt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem. He did that which was evil in Yahweh his God’s sight.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came up against him, and bound him in fetters to carry him to Babylon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nebuchadnezzar also carried some of the vessels of Yahweh’s house to Babylon, and put them in his temple at Babylon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the rest of the acts of Jehoiakim, and his abominations which he did, and that which was found in him, behold, they are written in the book of the kings of Israel and Judah; and Jehoiachin his son reigned in his place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jehoiachin was eight years old when he began to reign, and he reigned three months and ten days in Jerusalem. He did that which was evil in Yahweh’s sight.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At the return of the year, king Nebuchadnezzar sent and brought him to Babylon, with the valuable vessels of Yahweh’s house, and made Zedekiah his brother king over Judah and Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he began to reign, and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He did that which was evil in Yahweh his God’s sight. He didn’t humble himself before Jeremiah the prophet speaking from Yahweh’s mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also rebelled against king Nebuchadnezzar, who had made him swear by God; but he stiffened his neck, and hardened his heart against turning to Yahweh, the God of Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Moreover all the chiefs of the priests, and the people, trespassed very greatly after all the abominations of the nations; and they polluted Yahweh’s house which he had made holy in Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yahweh, the God of their fathers, sent to them by his messengers, rising up early and sending, because he had compassion on his people, and on his dwelling place;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but they mocked the messengers of God, and despised his words, and scoffed at his prophets, until Yahweh’s wrath arose against his people, until there was no remedy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore he brought on them the king of the Chaldeans, who killed their young men with the sword in the house of their sanctuary, and had no compassion on young man or virgin, old man or gray-headed. He gave them all into his hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the vessels of God’s house, great and small, and the treasures of Yahweh’s house, and the treasures of the king, and of his princes, all these he brought to Babylon.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They burned God’s house, and broke down the wall of Jerusalem, and burned all its palaces with fire, and destroyed all of its valuable vessels.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He carried those who had escaped from the sword away to Babylon, and they were servants to him and his sons until the reign of the kingdom of Persia,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to fulfill Yahweh’s word by Jeremiah’s mouth, until the land had enjoyed its Sabbaths. As long as it lay desolate, it kept Sabbath, to fulfill seventy years.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now in the first year of Cyrus king of Persia, that Yahweh’s word by the mouth of Jeremiah might be accomplished, Yahweh stirred up the spirit of Cyrus king of Persia, so that he made a proclamation throughout all his kingdom, and put it also in writing, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Thus says Cyrus king of Persia, ‘Yahweh, the God of heaven, has given all the kingdoms of the earth to me; and he has commanded me to build him a house in Jerusalem, which is in Judah. Whoever there is among you of all his people, Yahweh his God be with him, and let him go up.’”
